Description: In Washington, the United States (US),  the Secretary of State Antony Blinken spoke with the Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov to follow up on US written response to Russia’s security proposals. Further he reiterated US support to Ukraine's sovereignty and warning of severe consequences in the event of if Russia invades Ukraine, State Government reported. Source link : https://www.state.gov/secretary-blinkens-call-with-russian-foreign-minister-lavrov-6/
Headline: US Secretary of State with the Russian Foreign Minister in the United States' Washington

Description: In Washington D.C, the United States, the US State Department ordered family members of US embassy employees operating in Belarus to leave the country and further issued a travel advisory for US nationals from visiting Belarus over the latter's close ties with Russia, amid ongoing Russian military buildup along Ukraine's border, the Hill reported. Source link : https://thehill.com/policy/international/592262-state-department-orders-embassy-employees-families-to-leave-belarus
Headline: US State Department of State orders its nationals to leave from Belarus in the United States' Washington

Description: In Washington, the United states, Secretary of State Antony Blinken spoke with the NATO Secretary-General Jens Stoltenberg, OSCE Chairman-in-Office Zbigniew Rau, and EU High Representative Josep Borrell to follow-up on progress related to Russian military build up along Ukraine's border, among others, State Governemnt reported. Source link : https://www.state.gov/secretary-blinkens-call-with-nato-secretary-general-stoltenberg-osce-chair-in-office-and-polish-foreign-minister-rau-and-eu-high-representative-borrell/

Description: In Moscow, Russia, President Vladimir Putin stated that the United States response on Russia's security guarantees had ignored three Russian major concerns. Russia's concerns included North Atlantic Treaty Organization's (NATO's) possible eastward expansion, particularly into Ukraine and Georgia, the continued deployment of weapon systems near Russia, and NATO's expansion beyond its 1997 footprint, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/politics/01/02/2022/61f962d39a79479a1bec32ef?from=from_main_1
Headline: Russia's President states that US ignored its concerns

Description: In Brussels, Belgium, the European Commission formally initiated a proposal to distribute EUR1.2 billion of a macro financial assistance to Ukraine, if approved by the Council and the European Parliament, the commission would immediately transfer the first EUR600 million, EC Europa reported. Source link : https://ec.europa.eu/commission/presscorner/detail/en/ip_22_674
Headline: European Commission proposes to financially assist Ukraine

Description: In Washington, United States, Department of State ordered evacuation of its embassy staff and their dependents from Belarus due to 'unusual and concerning Russian military activity near the border with Ukraine'. It also issued a public warning against travel to the country, Zerkalo reported. Source link : https://news.zerkalo.io/economics/9348.html
Headline: US Department of State orders evacuation of its staff from Belarus

Description: In Ottawa, Canada, Defense Minister Anita Anand, during a meeting with NATO Secretary-General Jens Stoltenberg, expressed the nation's support to impose economic sanctions on Russia in the event of Russian invasion in Ukraine and reaffirmed plans to expand its training mission in Ukraine, Reuters reported. Source link : https://www.reuters.com/world/canada-nato-says-russia-has-choice-between-de-escalation-sanctions-2022-02-01/

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, National Security and Defence Council (NSDC) secretary Alexei Danilov urged France and Germany to not force Ukraine for the implementation of the Minsk agreement on the Donbas conflict settlement, as its fulfilment could deteriorate the domestic political situation in the country, DW reported. Source link : https://www.dw.com/ru/v-kieve-schitajut-chto-vypolnenie-minskih-soglashenij-unichtozhit-ukrainu/a-60616843

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, United Kingdom's Prime Minister Boris Johnson met with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to discuss Russian hostilities, including military buildup near Ukrainian territory. Johnson reiterated UK’s support for Ukrainian sovereignty and territorial integrity and the leaders warned of the humanitarian cost of a Russian invasion, Government UK reported. Source link : https://www.gov.uk/government/news/uk-ukraine-joint-leaders-statement-1-february-2022
Headline: UK Prime Minister meets Ukraine's President to discuss Russian hostilities

Description: In Ottawa, Canada, the federal government updated its travel advisory and urged its citizens to avoid all kinds of travel to Ukraine, amid Russian military build-up along Ukraine's borders. The government had initially asked Canadians to avoid only non-essential travel to Ukraine, Global News reported. Source link : https://globalnews.ca/news/8586376/avoid-all-travel-to-ukraine-canada-russia-tensions/
Headline: Government update its travel advisory to Ukraine in Canada's Ottawa

Description: In Washington, the United States (US), Senator Josh Hawley (Republican-Montana) urged the Biden administration to stop the support to Ukraine in its bid for NATO membership and stated that such a move would distract the US from its focus on China's increasing presence in the Indo-Pacific region, the Hill reported. Source link : https://thehill.com/policy/defense/592431-hawley-calling-on-biden-admin-to-drop-support-for-ukraines-eventual-membership
Headline: Senator urges the Biden administration to limit the support to Ukraine in the United States' Washington

Description: In Washington, the United States, President Joe Biden spoke with his French counterpart Emmanuel Macron and reaffirmed support for Ukraine’s sovereignty despite the Russian military buildup on Ukraine's borders. They agreed to resolve the issue through diplomacy and prepare for economic sanctions if diplomacy fails, White House reported. Source link : https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefing-room/statements-releases/2022/02/02/readout-of-president-joseph-r-biden-jr-s-call-with-president-emmanuel-macron-of-france-2/

Description:

Description:
In Moscow, Russia, French President Emmanuel Macron met Russian President Vladimir Putin to de-escalate the situation with Ukraine, Reuters reported.

Description: In London, England province, United Kingdom, Prime Minister Alexander Boris de Pfeffel Johnson held talks with the leaders of Canada, the United States, Italy, France, Germany, the European Union, Romania, Poland and North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) to discuss enhanced sanctions to counter Russian aggression towards Ukraine. Source link :

 Source link :

 Zelenskyy compared Russia's invasion to Holocaust, plead for more support, and and criticized Israel's limitations on accepting non-Jewish Ukrainian refugees, Wisconsin Public Radio reported. Source link : https://www.wpr.org/zelenskyy-compares-russias-invasion-holocaust-plea-israel-support

Description: In Brussels, Belgium, the European Union (EU) banned Russia’s state-controlled media outlets, RT and Sputnik, and their subsidiaries in a new round of sanctions imposed on Russia in response for its invasion of Ukraine, the Guardian reported. Source link : https://www.theguardian.com/media/2022/feb/27/eu-ban-russian-state-backed-channels-rt-sputnik
Headline: EU bans Russian media outlets in a new round of sanctions

Description: In Vienna, Austria, Minister of the Interior Gerhard Karner announced that approximately 45,000 people fleeing from the military conflict in Ukraine had entered Austrian territory, Der Standard reported. Source link : https://www.derstandard.at/story/2000133894169/bisher-rund-45-000-menschen-aus-ukraine-nach-oesterreich-geflohen
Headline: Austria reports on the arrival of Ukrainian refugees

Description: In London, England, United Kingdom, the government froze assets of 350 Russian nationals and banned them from travelling to the country after they were allegedly implicated in the Russian decision to invade Ukraine, Al Jazeera reported Source link : https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/3/15/uk-blacklists-350-more-russians-imposes-new-import-tariffs
Headline: UK freeze assets of 350 more Russian nationals

Description: In Oslo, Norway, the Norwegian spokesperson of the International Legion of Territorial Defence of Ukraine Damien Magrou said that none of their fighters in the international legion were wounded in the attack by Russia on Javoriv. The international legion is a unit of the Ukrainian Armed Forces which comprises an unknown number of foreign fighters, BBCM reported. Source link : https://monitoring.bbc.co.uk/product/c203c18h
Headline: Norway denies any of their soldier being killed in the Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description:
In Brussels, Belgium, the European Union (EU) approved USD538450000 to Ukraine amid Russian invasion of the country, the European Council reported.
 Source link : https://www.consilium.europa.eu/en/press/press-releases/2022/03/23/eu-support-to-ukraine-council-doubles-funding-under-the-european-peace-facility/
Headline: EU approves financial aid to Ukraine amid Russian invasion

Description: In Tokyo, Japan, Japanese Senator Munyo Suzuki criticised United States (US) President Joe Biden's continued incitement to his Ukrainian counterpart Volodymyr Zelenskyy. He stressed that the US President should inform Zelensky that the outcome of the situation in Ukraine has become completely clear and there is no other option but to dialogue with Russia, Sana reported. Source link : https://sana.sy/?p=1613878
Headline: Japanese Senator criticises US President for continuous incitement to his Ukrainian President

Description: In Kyiv city, Kyvi Oblast, Ukraine, Viktor Zhora, Deputy Chairman of the State Service for Special Communications and Information Protection (Gosspetssvyaz) of Ukraine announced that, government developed a cyber contingency plan to transfer the country’s public sensitive data to back-up data storage facilities outside Kyiv and to wipe its Kyiv-based computer servers in the event of Russia’s military invasion in the capital, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/politics/23/02/2022/6215b6839a7947fda3664696?from=newsfeed
Headline: Ukrainian government official announces contingency plan to defend against cyber-attack in case of Russia invasion

Description: In Minsk city, Minsk oblast, Belarus, President Alexander Lukashenko said that Russia's invasion of Ukraine would soon end with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy accepting the terms imposed by Russia, Reformation reported. Source link : https://reform.by/303575-lukashenko-zelenskij-ili-soglasitsja-na-uslovija-rossii-ili-podpishet-akt-o-kapituljacii
Headline: Belarusian President briefs on the Russia's invasion of Ukraine

Description: In Warsaw, Mazowieckie province, Poland, Prime Minister Mateusz Morawiecki discussed with Moldovan Prime Minister Natalia Gavrilita the effect of the war in Ukraine on their countries' situation, including their security, economies and finances. The report added that both leaders also discussed the coordination of humanitarian aid for Ukraine, the First News reported. Source link : https://www.thefirstnews.com/article/polish-belgian-moldovan-pms-discuss-aid-for-ukraine-28459
Headline: Polish PM discusses with Moldovan PM the effect of the war in Ukraine on their countries

Description: In Ankara city, Ankara province, Turkey, the Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan announced that the Turkish government will join sanctions against Russians over the invasion of Ukraine, Prensa Latina reported. Source link : https://www.prensa-latina.cu/?post_type=post&p=209312
Headline: Turkey rules out joining sanctions against Russia in Turkey's Ankara

Description: In Addis Ababa city, Addis Ababa Province, Ethiopia, Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed called for both sides in the Russia-Ukrainian conflict to exercise restraint, Borkena reported. Source link : https://borkena.com/2022/03/03/ethiopia-ukraine-conflict-abiy-ahmed-eritrea/
Headline: Ethiopian government calls for restrain in Russian-Ukrainian conflict

Description: In Odessa city, Odessa Oblast, Russia, the Bulgarian government evacuated its officials of the Bulgarian consular mission in Odessa due to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, BNR reported. Source link : https://bnr.bg/en/post/101608581
Headline: Bulgarian government evacuate its officials from Ukraine's Odessa Oblast

Description: In Kyiv City, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, the central government eased administrative and border control restrictions to streamline the cross-border transportation of humanitarian assistance to Ukraine, Pravda reported. Source link : https://www.epravda.com.ua/rus/news/2022/03/2/683115/
Headline: Ukrainian government eases administrative and border control restrictions for humanitarian assistance

Description: In Kyiv city, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, the Ukrainian Presidential Chief of Staff Andriy Yermak announced that Israel was mediating in the Russia-Ukraine conflict. He said, "Israel has committed itself to the complicated but noble mission of mediator in search of peace and ending Russia's aggression against Ukraine," Facebook reported. Source link :
Headline: Ukraine official announces that Israel is mediating in Russia-Ukraine conflict

Description: In Doha, Qatar, the Qatari Minister of Foreign Affairs Mohammed bin Abdul Rahman Al Thani and the Ukrainian Minister of Foreign Affairs Dmytro Kuleba discussed developments in the Russian invasion of the Ukraine over a phone call, BBC Monitoring reported. Source link : https://monitoring.bbc.co.uk/product/c203bjr5
Headline: Qatari and Ukrainian officials discuss Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In Berlin city, Berlin State, Germany, Minister of Transport Volker Wissing announced that the national railway company Deutsche Bahn (DB) was to transport relief supplies to Ukraine. This statement comes after launching a nationwide collection drive of humanitarian goods in light of the tense situation on the Polish-Ukrainian border, Frankfurter Allgemeine reported. Source link : https://www.faz.net/agenturmeldungen/dpa/wissing-hilfsgueter-kommen-mit-zuegen-in-die-ukraine-17855967.html
Headline: German government transports humanitarian aid to Ukraine

Description: In Beirut city, Beirut Governorate, Lebanon, Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah claimed that the United States (US) Embassy in Lebanon was responsible for the Lebanese Ministry of Foreign Affairs' statement condemning Russia's invasion of Ukraine, arguing it was in Lebanon's interest to remain neutral regarding this war, An Nahar reported. Source link : https://www.annahar.com/arabic/section/76-%D8%B3%D9%8A%D8%A7%D8%B3%D8%A9/08032022041824602
Headline: Hezbollah claims US is responsible for Lebanese government's condemnation of Russia

Description: In Kyiv city, Kyiv oblast, Ukraine, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy suggested that Ukrainians deserve a peace deal with Russia that ensures 'honest security guarantees', BBC Monitoring reported. Source link :
Headline: Ukrainian President suggests Ukrainians deserve a peace deal with Russia

Description: In Kyiv city, Kyiv oblast, Ukraine, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy proposed in a speech to the United States (US) Congress to establish an association called United for Peace (U24) with a mandate to provide various assistance, including weapon supplies, to conflict-affected countries, Al Jazeera reported. Source link : https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/3/16/what-zelenskyy-said-to-the-us-congress
Headline: Ukrainian President proposes to establish an association for peace

Description: In Kyiv city, Kyiv oblast, Ukraine, the Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced that he will insist on sovereignty and territorial integrity in the next round of peace talks with Russia, El Mostrador reported. Source link : https://www.elmostrador.cl/destacado/2022/03/28/zelenski-insistira-en-integridad-territorial-en-proxima-ronda-de-conversaciones-con-rusia-mientras-ucrania-exporta-primer-embarque-de-granos-por-tren-a-europa/
Headline: Ukrainian President to insist on territorial integrity in next round of talks with Russia

Description: In Berlin city, Berlin region, Germany, the Chancellor Olaf Sholz rejected that regime change in Russia is 'North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO's) objective' despite the invasion of Ukraine, La Jornada reported. Source link : https://www.jornada.com.mx/notas/2022/03/27/mundo/cambio-de-regimen-en-rusia-no-es-objetivo-de-la-otan-sholz/
Headline: German Chancellor rejects claims of regime change in Russia is an objective in Germany's Berlin

Description: In Beirut city, Beirut Governorate, Lebanon, Minister of Labour Mostafa Bayram during a cabinet meeting denounced the Ministry of Foreign Affairs statements on Russia's invasion of Ukraine. He argued that the Council of Minister should have discussed Lebanon's position beforehand. Lebanon had condemned Russia's invasion of Ukraine, Aljadeed reported. Source link : https://www.aljadeed.tv/arabic/news/local/0403202254
Headline: Lebanese minister questions government stand on Russian-Ukrainian conflict

Description: In Sofia city, Sofia city Province, Bulgaria, the General Directorate Border Police reported that so far some 25,000 Ukrainian refugees had entered Bulgaria via the Romanian-Bulgarian border since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, BNR reported. Source link : https://bnr.bg/en/post/101611148/25000-refugees-fleeing-from-ukraine-have-entered-bulgaria
Headline: Bulgaria reports 25,000 refugees fled Ukraine and entered its territory

Description: In Bratislava city, Bratislava region, Slovakia, the Minister of Foreign Affairs Yair Lapid announced that the Government of Israel would abide by the sanctions against Russia. The sanctions were forced against Russia over the invasion of Ukraine, Egypt Independent reported. Source link :
Headline: Israeli official announces to abide by sanctions against Russia during his visit to Slovakia

Description: In Kremlin, Moskva oblast, Russian Federation, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs warned against aggressive actions towards Russians in foreign country. Ministry alleged the discrimination campaigns against Russian students, athletes and cultural and arts figures due to Russia's invasion of Ukraine, BBC Monitoring reported. Source link :
Headline: Russian government warns against the discrimination towards Russians in foreign country

Description: In Bucharest city, București region, Romania, the Minister of Interior Lucian Bode reported that so far 172,935 Ukrainian refugees had entered Romania. He further added that 1,897 refugees had applied for asylum in Romania since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, Ager Pres reported. Source link : https://www.agerpres.ro/agerpres/2022/03/04/172-935-ukrainians-enter-romania-since-conflict-starts-58-106-remain-1-897-seeking-asylum--877879
Headline: Romania reports 172,935 refugees fled Ukraine and entered its territory

Description: In London, England, United Kingdom, Prime Minister Alexander Boris de Pfeffel Johnson introduces a new scheme called 'Homes for Ukraine'. The scheme will let refugees from the war come to United Kingdom even if they do not have family ties, Swiss Info reported. Source link :
Headline: UK Prime Minister announces to help Ukrainian refugees

Description: In Washington D.C, the United States (US), White House Press Secretary Jen Psaki reported that President Joe Biden agreed to meet his Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin in principle if Russia didn't move forward to invade Ukraine, the Hill reported. Source link : https://thehill.com/homenews/administration/595124-biden-willing-to-meet-with-putin-if-russia-does-not-invade-ukraine
Headline: US President to meet his Russian counterpart in the United States' Washington

Description: In Washington D.C, the United States, Vice President Kamala Harris held a meeting with Baltic country leaders, including Lithuania President Gitanas Nauseda. The meeting was held to discuss a unified approach to respond to Russian military build-up along Ukraine's border. They also discussed North Atlantic Treaty Organisation (NATO) partnership and imposition of sanctions if Russia invades Ukraine, the White House reported. Source link : https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefing-room/statements-releases/2022/02/18/readout-of-vice-president-kamala-harriss-meeting-with-baltic-leaders/
Headline: US Vice President holds a meeting with Baltic country leaders

Description: In Washington D.C, the United States, President Joe Biden spoke with Transatlantic leaders, including Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen and French President Emmanuel Macron, to discuss developments related to the Russian military build-up along Ukraine's border, the White House reported. Source link : https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefing-room/statements-releases/2022/02/18/readout-of-president-bidens-call-with-transatlantic-leaders/
Headline: US President discusses Ukraine crisis with Transatlantic leaders

Description: In Washington D. C, the United States, Secretary of State Antony Blinken suspected Russian President Vladimir Putin with plans to launch an invasion into Ukraine, despite several efforts of nations across the world to diffuse the situation, the Hill reported. Source link : https://thehill.com/homenews/administration/595089-blinken-putin-is-moving-forward-with-plan-to-invade-ukraine
Headline: Secretary of State suspects Russia plans to invade Ukraine in the United States' Washington

Description: In Washington D.C, the United States, President Joe Biden held a meeting with the National Security Council to discuss the developments related to the Russian military build-up along Ukraine's border, amid reports of Russia planning to invade Ukraine, White House Press Release reported. Source link : https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefing-room/statements-releases/2022/02/20/readout-of-president-bidens-national-security-council-meeting/
Headline: President holds a meeting with the National Security Council in the United States' Washington

Description: In Washington D.C, the United States, President Joe Biden issued an executive order to bar new investments in the Donetsk People's Republic (DPR) and Luhansk People's Republic (LPR) regions of Ukraine, continue to impose sanctions on certain officials in the Covered Regions, and prevent certain transactions, amid Russian military build-up along Ukraine's borders, White House Press Release reported. Source link : https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefing-room/statements-releases/2022/02/21/letter-to-the-speaker-of-the-house-of-representatives-and-the-president-of-the-senate-regarding-the-blocking-property-of-certain-persons-and-prohibiting-certain-transactions-with-respect-to-continued/
Headline: US President issues order to bar new investments in Ukraine's separatist regions

Description: In Washington D. C, the United States (US), the US Department of State announced plans to relocate its embassy operations from Ukraine to Poland and added that embassy personnel would return on a regular basis to Ukraine to provide emergency consular services, amid reports of possible Russian invasion in Ukraine, the Hill reported. Source link : https://thehill.com/policy/international/595251-us-relocates-ukraine-embassy-staff-to-poland
Headline: Department of State announces plans to relocate its embassy operations in the United States' Washington

Description: In Washington D.C, United States, White House Press Secretary Jen Psaki confirmed that President Joe Biden would not hold a scheduled meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin unless Russia de-escalates the situation in eastern Ukraine, the Hill reported. Source link : https://thehill.com/homenews/administration/595399-psaki-confirms-biden-putin-meeting-off-the-table
Headline: US government cancels meeting with Russian President

Description: In Washington D.C, the United States (US), President Joe Biden announced to impose sanctions on Russia. This would include sovereign debt, elites and their family members, financial institutions, Russian state bank Promsvyazbank. The decision was made due to Russia's advancement of troops in eastern Ukraine border, which the US categorised as the start of an invasion, the Hill reported. Source link : https://thehill.com/homenews/administration/595356-biden-announces-initial-sanctions-on-russia-over-troop-deployments-in
Headline: US President announces sanctions on Russia

Description: In the United Nations, New York, United States, the Kenyan Ambassador Martin Kimani criticised Russia for using military force against Ukraine, He stated that Russian President Vladimir Putin had rejected diplomacy in favor of military force National Public Radio reported. Source link :
Headline: Kenya criticises Russia for invading Ukraine

Description: In the United Nations, New York, United States, the South African Foreign Minister Naledi Pandor stated that Russia must immediately withdraw its forces from Ukraine, The Foreign Minister also expressed her support for the Minsk agreements and called for resumption of diplomatic efforts to find a solution to the crisis, Africa Times reported. Source link :
Headline: South African Foreign Minister calls for Russian withdrawal from Ukraine

Description: In Washington, D.C., District of Columbia, United States, Deputy Secretary of State Wendy Sherman spoke with North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) Deputy Secretary-General Mircea Geoana, European External Action Service  Secretary-General Stefano Sannino and others to discuss plans to impose stricter sanctions on Russia and urge Russia to withdraw its troops from Ukraine, US Department of State reported. Source link : https://www.state.gov/deputy-secretary-shermans-call-with-nato-deputy-secretary-general-geoana-the-osce-chair-in-office-represented-by-polish-deputy-foreign-minister-przydacz-osce-senior-adviser-hart-and-eeas-s/
Headline: Deputy Secretary of State and European officials discuss imposition of stricter sanctions on Russia

Description: In New York City, New York, United States, the Brazilian Ambassador to the United Nations (UN), Ronaldo Costa Filho, avoided direct criticism of Russia for the fire caused at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant in Ukraine. He urged moderation on both sides to end the conflict during the emergency meeting of the UN Security Council, Aracaju Agora News reported Source link : https://www1.folha.uol.com.br/mundo/2022/03/brasil-evita-criticas-a-russia-em-reuniao-sobre-ataque-a-usina-nuclear-na-ucrania.shtml?origin=uol
Headline: Brazilian Ambassador to the United Nations avoids direct criticism of Russia

Description: In Washington D.C, United States (US), in a call with Ukrainian Foreign Minister Dmytro Kuleba, the Secretary of State Antony Blinken discussed several diplomatic efforts carried out by the US and its allies in combatting Russia's invasion in Ukraine. He further reaffirmed US commitment to Ukraine's sovereignty, US Department of State reported. Source link : https://www.state.gov/secretary-blinkens-call-with-ukrainian-foreign-minister-kuleba-13/
Headline: Government officials from US and Ukraine discuss Russian invasion

Description: In Washington, District of Colombia, United States, Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schume said the legislation to revoke Russia's Permanent Normal Trade Relations status following its attack on Ukraine will move through the US Senate promptly, following imminent passage by the House of Representatives, Jerusalem Post reported. Source link : https://www.jpost.com/breaking-news/article-701603
Headline: US announces to pass bill revoking Russia's preferential trade status.

Description:
In New York, United States, the Serbian delegation voted in favour of a resolution at the United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) that urges Russia to end its invasion against Ukraine, N1 Info reported. 
 Source link : https://rs.n1info.com/english/news/serbia-votes-to-adopt-new-un-general-assembly-resolution/
Headline: Serbia votes in favour of UN resolution that urges Russia to end its invasion against Ukraine

Description: At the United Nations General Assembly, New York, United States, during the United Nations General Assembly on Ukraine, Syria's permanent representative to the United Nations Ambassador Bassam Sabbagh affirmed Syria's keenness to ensure the preservation of human rights and the provision of necessary humanitarian assistance to all crisis areas. He also said that humanitarian issues should not be politicized, Sana reported. Source link : https://sana.sy/?p=1611430
Headline: Syria expresses keenness for humanitarian assistance in Ukraine

Description: In Moscow, the Russian Federation, the embassy of United States (US) in Russia issued a warning to its nationals and urged them to avoid crowded places in Moscow and other cities, amid growing threats of Russian invasion of Ukraine, the Hill reported. Source link : https://thehill.com/policy/international/595136-us-embassy-warns-americans-to-avoid-crowds-in-moscow-citing-threats-of
Headline: US embassy issues a warning to its nationals in Russia's Moscow

Description: A tLuzhniki stadium, Moscow, Russia, President Vladimir Putin promised tens of thousands of people that all the Kremlin’s aims would be achieved in Ukraine, Al Arabiya reported. Source link : https://english.alarabiya.net/News/world/2022/03/18/Putin-tells-mass-rally-that-Russia-will-prevail-in-Ukraine-
Headline: Russian President tells mass rally that Russia will prevail in Ukraine

Description: In Moscow, Russia, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs expelled United States (US) diplomats from the country. It stated that the move came in response to the US' expulsion of Russian diplomats from the United Nations (UN) Mission in New York and Russian employees of the United Nations Secretariat amid Russian invasion of Ukraine, Youm7 reported. Source link : https://www.youm7.com/story/2022/3/24/موسكو-تعلن-طرد-دبلوماسيين-أمريكيين-من-روسيا/5703221
Headline: Russia expels US diplomats

Description: In Buenos Aires, Argentina, Ukraine's Chargé d'affaires Sergiy Nebrat stated that he was not satisfied with the position of Argentina in relation to Russian invasion of Ukraine. He urged for economic sanctions against Russia and a signal from Argentina to ask Russian military forces to withdraw from Ukraine, La Nacion reported. Source link : https://www.lanacion.com.ar/el-mundo/guerra-rusia-ucrania-el-encargado-de-negocios-de-ucrania-en-buenos-aires-no-estamos-conformes-con-la-nid25022022/
Headline: Ukraine's ambassador denounces Argentina's position in Ukraine-Russia conflict

Description: In New Delhi, National Capital Territory of Delhi, India, Ministry of External Affairs denied reports of Indian nationals being held as hostages in Ukraine's Kharkiv amid Russian invasion of the country. The clarification came after the Russian government claimed that Ukrainian forces kept a large group of Indian students as hostages. In a press release, the ministry stated that it had requested Ukraine to arrange special trains for evacuation of its nationals. NDTV reported. Source link : https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/ukraine-crisis-india-says-have-not-received-any-reports-of-any-hostage-situation-regarding-any-student-after-russias-charge-on-ukraine-2800383
Headline: Indian government denies reports of Indian nationals being taken hostage in Ukraine

Description: In Moscow, Russia, Ministry of Defence  stated that Indian nationals were being held as hostages in Ukraine's Kharkiv city by the Ukrainian forces. The Indian government had earlier issued an urgent appeal to its nationals to immediately leave Kharkiv amid Russian invasion of Ukraine, NDTV reported. Source link : https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/indian-students-kept-as-hostages-by-ukrainians-in-kharkiv-says-russia-2800139
Headline: Russian ministry claims that Indian nationals were taken hostage in Ukraine

Description: At the Prime Minister's Office, New Delhi, National Capital Territory of Delhi, India, Prime Minister Narendra Modi met with the Russian Minister of Foreign Affairs Sergey Lavrov.  Modi reiterated his call for early cessation of violence and conveyed India's readiness to contribute to peace efforts. Lavrov briefed the prime minister on the situation in Ukraine, including the ongoing peace negotiations, and stated that Russia is open to India acting as a mediator in the Ukraine crisis, the Times Of India reported. Source link : https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/pm-modi-meets-russian-foreign-minister-says-india-ready-to-aid-peace-efforts-in-ukraine/articleshow/90595887.cms
Headline: Indian Prime Minister meets with Russian Minister of Foreign Affairs

Description:
In London, United Kingdom (UK), the government decided to allocate USD5.4 million to BBC World Service to tackle the disinformation amidst the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Ani News reported.
 Source link : https://aninews.in/news/world/europe/uk-to-provide-usd-54-million-to-tackle-disinformation-in-russia-ukraine20220324080628
Headline: UK to allocate funds to tackle disinformation amidst Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In Moscow, Russia, President Vladimir Putin said that the West aimed to damage the Russian economy but insisted that the country could withstand the sanctions. He said his country would achieve its goals in Ukraine and would not submit to what he called a Western attempt to achieve global dominance and dismember Russia, the New Times reported. Source link : https://www.newtimes.co.rw/international/putin-says-russia-will-achieve-ukraine-goals-decries-sanctions
Headline: Russian President says they will achieve their goals in Ukraine

Description: In Kremlin, Moskva oblast, Russian Federation, the Deputies of the Duma (Chamber of Deputies) approved the creation of a joint parliamentary commission proposed by the Federation Council (Senate) to investigate whether the laboratories that the United States had in Ukraine could be used to manufacture biological weapons against Russia, La Jornada reported. Source link : https://www.jornada.com.mx/notas/2022/03/23/mundo/aprueban-diputados-rusos-investigar-laboratorios-de-eu-en-ucrania/
Headline: Russian lawmakers approves investigation of US labs in Ukraine

Description: In Kyiv city, Kyiv oblast, Ukraine, the Ukrainian Ministry of Foreign Affairs warned that 'no country in the world', will recognize the result of a referendum such as the one they intend to carry out in the self-proclaimed separatist republic of Luhansk, El Caribe reported. Source link : https://www.elcaribe.com.do/destacado/ucrania-afirma-que-ningun-pais-reconocera-un-referendum-ilegal-en-donbas/
Headline: Ministry of Foreign Affairs warns against the referendum of republic of Luhansk in Ukraine

Description: At United Nations Headquarters, New York State, United States (US), the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) held an emergency meeting to discuss Russia’s military attack on and seizure of administrative control over Ukraine’s Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant. Ambassador of Ukraine to United Nations Sergiy Kyslytsya affirmed that the attack on Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant was an attack on entire Europe, PBS reported Source link : https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/watch-live-un-security-council-holds-emergency-meeting-after-russian-attack-on-ukrainian-nuclear-plant
Headline: UN Security Council discusses Russia’s military attack on Ukraine’s nuclear power plant

Description:
At Black Sea Port, Mykolaiv city, Odessa Oblast, Ukraine, the Syrian government evacuated 13 sailors of Syrian origin who had been aboard a
cargo ship sailing under the Lebanese flag. Syrian sailors were evacuated to Moldova and then to Romania in coordination with the
country's consulate in the nearby city of Odessa, Rusiya Al-Yawm reported. Source link :
Headline: Syrian government evacuates its sailors from Ukraine's Odessa Oblast

Description: In United Nations General Assembly (UNGA), New York State, United States (US), the Permanent Representative to the United Nations (UN) Bassam al-Sabbagh voted against the UN resolution in UNGA against Russia to immediately withdraw its forces from Ukraine. Syrian representative stated, 'the draft clearly represents a prejudiced attitude based on political propaganda fuelled by political pressure. Language against the Russian attempts to belittle its right to protect its people and its security concerns', United Nations reported. Source link :
Headline: Syria votes against UN resolution which calls for Russia to immediately withdraw its forces from Ukraine

Description: In Kremlin, Moscow city, Moskva Oblast, Russian Federation, the Qatari Minister of Foreign Affairs r Mohammed bin Abdul Rahman Al Thani announced that the Qatar government would like to push of peaceful resolution of Russia-Ukraine conflict. He said in his speech, 'Doha will provide all that can support and speed up a peaceful solution through negotiations,' Al Jazeera reported. Source link :
Headline: Qatari government official announces to push for a peaceful solution in Russia-Ukraine conflict

Description: In Jerusalem city, Jerusalem District, Israel, Ministry of Foreign Affairs confirmed that an Israeli citizen died from gunfire in Ukraine while travelling toward the border with Moldova, Y Net reported. Source link : https://www.ynet.co.il/news/article/skl8cdql9
Headline: Israeli government confirms the death of an Israeli national in Ukraine

Description: In Buenos Aires, Argentina, Minister of Foreign Affairs Santiago Cafiero stated that 84 Argentinian nationals left Ukraine due to Russia's invasion of Ukraine, Telam reported. Source link : https://www.telam.com.ar/notas/202203/585679-ucrania-argentinas-argentinos-gestiones-diplomaticas.html
Headline: Argentine Foreign Minister states the situation of its nationals in Ukraine

Description: In Moscow, Russia, the Investigative Committee of Russia opened a criminal case against prominent Russian journalist Alexander Nevzorov under the new censorship law adopted after Russian invasion of Ukraine. The new law imposes a jail term of up to 15 years for spreading fake news intentionally about the country's military. The investigation stated that the journalist reported about a deliberate shelling by the Russian Armed Forces at a maternity hospital in Mariupol, BBCM reported. Source link : https://monitoring.bbc.co.uk/product/c203c2sk
Headline: Russia opens a criminal case against a journalist

Description: In Canberra, Australian Capital Territory, Australia, both Australia and the Netherlands launched legal action against Russia in International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO) for downing the Malaysia Airlines Flight MH17 in Ukrainian airspace in 2014, an attack that left 298 civilians dead. Australia and Netherlands decided to take legal action against Russia to maximise pressure on the country after it invaded Ukraine, including on its struggling civil aviation sector, Stuff reported. Source link :
Headline: Australia and the Netherlands launch legal action against Russia at ICAO

Description: In the Hague, Zuid-Holland, Netherlands, the International Court of Justice (ICJ) issued a binding decision ordering Russia to immediately suspend its invasion of Ukraine. Court ordered to ensure the halting of fighting by Russia-backed militias in Ukraine, Al Jazeera reported. Source link : https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/3/16/un-court-orders-russia-to-cease-military-operations-in-ukraine
Headline: International court orders Russia to immediately suspend its invasion of Ukraine

Description: In the Hague, the Netherlands, the International Court of Justice (ICJ), the principal judicial organ of the United Nations, announced that they will hold public hearings in the Allegations of Genocide under the Convention on the Prevention and Punishment of the Crime of Genocide (Ukraine v. Russian Federation) case on 7 and 8 March 2022. This came after Ukraine filed a claim on 26 February 2022 at the international court amid Russia's invasion in the country. Ukrainian government said Russia “falsely claimed that acts of genocide have occurred” in the Donetsk People’s Republic (DPR) and Luhansk People’s Republic (LPR) and "on the basis of this false allegation, Russia is now engaged in a military invasion of Ukraine involving grave and widespread violations of the human rights of the Ukrainian people", the International Court of Justice reported. Source link :
Headline: ICJ announces public hearings in the allegations of genocide by Ukraine on Russia case

Description: In Kyiv, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, State Bureau of Investigations (SBI) raided the homes of unspecified number of Members of Parliament (MPs) from the pro-Russian parliamentary Opposition Platform-For Life (Опозиційна платформа — За життя: OPZZh) who illegally crossed or planned to cross the Ukrainian border. They seized three guns and unspecified amounts of cash in hryvnias and dollars from MP Vadym Rabinovych, two grenades from MP Viktor Chorny, and a pistol from MP Oleh Voloshyn, Interfax reported. Source link :
Headline: Security forces raid houses of pro-Russian MPs in Ukraine's Kyiv

Description: At Yahodyn border checkpoint, Volyn Oblast, Ukraine, the Armed Forces of Ukraine seized 25 cargo vehicles with Belarusian number plates trying to leave the country, at the Ukraine-Poland state border. Russian troops are coming from the territory of Belarus and missiles are being launched into Ukraine, Euro Radio reported. Source link : https://euroradio.fm/ru/ukraincy-izyali-25-belorusskih-fur-pri-popytke-vyezda-iz-strany
Headline: Ukraine’s army seizes Belarusian vehicles at the Ukraine-Poland state border

Description: In Manhattan, New York, United States, the Brazilian Ambassador to the United Nations, Ronaldo Costa Filho, refrained from direct criticism of Russia for the fire caused at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant in Ukraine. He urged both sides to follow restraint and end the conflict during the emergency meeting, Folha de S.Paulo reported. Source link : https://www1.folha.uol.com.br/mundo/2022/03/brasil-evita-criticas-a-russia-em-reuniao-sobre-ataque-a-usina-nuclear-na-ucrania.shtml?origin=uol
Headline: Brazil's ambassador to the UN refrains from direct criticism of Russia

Description: In Warsaw, Poland, at the Bucharest Nine summit consisting of nine countries, Poland's President Andrzej Duda announced that they should 'support Ukraine with weapons and all kinds of assistance'. President Duda added that ''the world needs to respond to Russia'' after Russian invasion of Ukraine, Ukrinform reported. Source link :
Headline: Poland's President announces support for Ukraine

Description: In Warsaw, Mazowieckie province, Poland, President Andrzej Duda urged to introduce tougher sanctions against Russia in response to Russia’s attack on a maternity hospital in Ukraine. He referred to the attack as an 'act of barbarity and genocide', CNN reported. Source link : https://edition.cnn.com/europe/live-news/ukraine-russia-putin-news-03-10-22/h_a13456e950b63a00912714edbf05f721
Headline: Poland urges to introduce tougher sanctions against Russia

Description: In Warsaw, Poland, President of the United States Joe Biden along with US Secretary of State Antony Blinken and US Defence Secretary Lloyd Austin met with the Minister of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine Dmytro Kuleba and Minister of Defence Oleksii Reznikov to discuss Russia's invasion of Ukraine, New Age Bangladesh reported. Source link : https://www.newagebd.net/article/166474/biden-meets-top-ukrainian-ministers-in-warsaw
Headline: US President meets Ukrainian Ministers in Poland's Warsaw

Description: In Tokyo, Japan, Prime Minister Fumio Kishida announced additional sanctions to build pressure on Russia after it invaded Ukraine. The new branch of sanctions include export controls on high-tech products such as semiconductors, a freeze on assets held by Russian financial institutions, and a suspension of visa issuance for certain Russian individuals and entities, Japan Times reported. Source link :
Headline: Japanese Prime Minister announces sanctions on Russia for invading Ukraine

Description: In Antalya, Turkey, during trilateral talks between Russia, Ukraine, and Turkey, Russian Minister of Foreign Affairs Sergey Lavrov refused to discuss a possible ceasefire regime in Ukraine, stating that this issue should remain solely on the agenda of the Russia-Ukraine peace talks taking place in Belarus, CNN reported. Source link : https://edition.cnn.com/europe/live-news/ukraine-russia-putin-news-03-10-22/h_a1c1e0e9af8cf5b88cca69434d5cafdd
Headline: Russia refuses to discuss a possible ceasefire regime in Ukraine

Description: In Antalya, Turkey, Russian Minister of Foreign Affairs Sergei Lavrov said Russia would not use nuclear weapons in Ukraine. He also cautioned the United States and Europe that Russia would never again want to be dependent on the West. This came after the West imposed heavy sanctions on almost the entire Russian financial and corporate system following the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Reuters reported. Source link : https://www.reuters.com/world/nuclear-war-russias-lavrov-says-i-dont-believe-so-2022-03-10/
Headline: Russia rules out the use of nuclear weapons in Ukraine

Description: In Antalya, Turkey, Russian Minister of Foreign Affairs Sergei Lavrov, and Venezuelan Vice-President Delcy Rodríguez held a meeting amid the armed conflict in Ukraine and the alleged rapprochement between the governments of the United States and Venezuela. This meeting came after the United States' officials proposed that Venezuela supply at least a portion of crude exports to the US as part of some agreement to ease trade sanctions on the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) member country, La Nacion reported. Source link : https://www.lanacion.com.ar/agencias/vicepresidenta-de-venezuela-sostuvo-encuentro-con-canciller-ruso-en-turquia-nid10032022/
Headline: Russia discusses armed conflict in Ukraine with Venezuela

Description: In Washington, D.C., District of Columbia, United States, Biden administration urged the Congress for an additional fund of USD6.4 billion to support Ukraine's economy, security and provide humanitarian assistance amid Russian military invasion. It also called for USD2.9  billion for the Defence Department and USD3.5 billion for Pentagon, the Hill reported. Source link : https://thehill.com/policy/international/595928-white-house-seeks-64b-from-congress-in-ukraine-related-aid
Headline: US President Biden calls for additional funding for Ukraine

Description: In Washington, D.C., District of Columbia, United States, Department of the Treasury sanctioned Russian President Vladimir Putin, Minister of Foreign Affairs Sergei Lavrov, Minister of Defense Sergei Shoigu and two other members of Russia’s Security Council who were directly responsible for Russia's further invasion of Ukraine, US Department of Treasury reported. Source link : https://www.state.gov/imposing-sanctions-on-president-putin-and-three-other-senior-russian-officials/
Headline: US Department of Treasury sanctions Russian President and other government officials

Description: In New York, United States, United Arab Emirates (UAE) abstained on the draft resolution prepared by the United States in the United Nations Security Council condemning the Russian invasion of Ukraine, EREM News reported. Source link :
Headline: UAE abstains from UNSC draft against Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In New York, United States, South Africa abstained United Nations (UN) procedural vote to adopt a resolution demanding Russia’s immediate and unconditional withdrawal from Ukraine. South Africa’s ambassador to the UN Mathu Joyini stated that the resolution in its current form could drive a wedge between the parties. South Africa explained its abstention of vote over the resolution’s lack of emphasis on a diplomatic path, SABC reported. Source link :
Headline: South Africa abstains from UN vote on emergency session on Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In New York, United States, Zimbabwe abstained United Nations (UN) procedural vote to adopt a resolution demanding Russia’s immediate and unconditional withdrawal from Ukraine. Minister of Foreign Affairs Amon Murwira stated that Zimbabwe believed it was the duty of the international community not to make the situation more complex than it already is. He also added that Zimbabwe is not convinced that the resolution will help in the direction of dialogue, Twitter account of Ministry of Information, Publicity & Broadcasting reported. Source link :
Headline: Zimbabwe abstains from UN vote on emergency session on Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In New York, United States, United Arab Emirates expressed concerns about the deteriorating humanitarian situation in Ukraine. UAE calls for restraint, a ceasefire and a peaceful resolution of the conflict. They also request the members of the United Nations to reach out to those in need and to refrain from targeting civilians, Permanent Mission of the United Arab Emirates to the United Nations reported. Source link :
Headline: United Arab Emirates express concern over deteriorating humanitarian situation in Ukraine

Description: In Ottawa, Ontario, Canada, the government announced a new immigration policy for Ukrainian nationals amid Russian invasion of the country. Ukraine, Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) launched two emergency routes for Ukrainian nationals to enter Canada: Canada-Ukraine Authorization for Emergency Travel for temporary stay and a special family reunification sponsorship pathway for permanent residence in Canada. All Ukrainian using these routes would be eligible to apply for open work permits, Government of Canada reported. Source link :
Headline: Canadian government announces new immigration policy for Ukrainian nationals

Description: In Washington, District of Columbia, United States, the US Senate supported a non-binding resolution condemning the Russian invasion of Ukraine and urging an investigation of Russian President Vladimir Putin and his allies for war crimes, Economic Times reported. Source link : https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/international/world-news/us-senate-approves-resolution-probing-russian-president-putin-for-war-crimes/articleshow/90247082.cms
Headline: US Senate supports a non-binding resolution condemning Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In Ottawa, Ontario, Canada, the Governor-General in Council Mary Simon on the recommendation of the Minister of Foreign Affairs introduced sanctions against 15 Russian public officials allegedly implicated in Russia's decision to invade Ukraine, the Government of Canada reported. Source link : https://www.international.gc.ca/world-monde/international_relations-relations_internationales/sanctions/russia_regulations-reglement_russie10.aspx?lang=eng
Headline: Canada sanctions 15 Russian public officials

Description: In Washington D.C., United States, U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken announced to travel to Israel and meet with Israeli Prime Minister Naftali Bennett. The meeting would primarily focus on Russian invasion of Ukraine, Devdiscourse reported. Source link : https://www.devdiscourse.com/article/politics/1978421-washingtons-top-diplomat-to-meet-with-israels-bennett
Headline: US Secretary of State plans a meeting with Israeli Prime Minister

Description: In Canberra, Australia, Prime Minister Scott Morrison announced to fund defensive weapons worth USD69 million for Ukraine. Additionally, the government announced to commit USD25 million in humanitarian support after the country was invaded by Russia, the Straits Times reported. Source link :
Headline: Australia approves funds for defense and humanitarian aid for Ukraine

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, the Egyptian embassy in the city urged its citizens to stay indoors after Ukraine imposed martial law, closed railways, and airways amid Russian invasion. They urges the citizens to keep their documents along and to follow Ukrainian authorities' instructions, Cairo24.com reported. Source link :
Headline: Egyptian embassy urges its citizens to stay indoors amid Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In Chisinau, Moldova, during a press conference United States' Secretary of State Antony Blinken announced that the US will continue to provide assistance to Ukraine. They were looking for airplanes that Poland may provide to Ukraine, Interfax reported. Source link :
Headline: United States provides further assistance to Ukraine

Description: In Kyiv, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, Lebanese Embassy in Ukraine announced that it facilitated the safe passage of Lebanese nationals toward Romania and Poland. They called on the European Union (EU) to provide them with temporary travelling documents, in the context of the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Aljadeed reported. Source link : https://www.aljadeed.tv/arabic/news/local/0303202210
Headline: Lebanon evacuates its nationals after Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In Kyiv, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Reintegration of the Temporarily Occupied Territories of Ukraine Irina Vereshchuk urged Russia to open humanitarian corridors from the Ukrainian cities of Mariupol and Pokrovsk in Donetsk region; Sumy, Kharkiv, Izyum, Balakliya, Lyptsi, and Strilka in Kherson region. She also urged for a ceasefire to evacuate civilians stuck between military clashes amid Russian invasion of the country, HB reported. Source link : https://nv.ua/ukraine/events/gumanitarnye-koridory-50222870.html
Headline: Ukraine's Deputy Prime Minister urges Russia to open humanitarian corridors

Description: In Kyiv, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced that Ukraine would continue talks with Russia to ensure the opening of humanitarian corridors for the evacuations of civilians from settlements occupied by Russian armed forces, CNN reported. Source link : https://edition.cnn.com/europe/live-news/ukraine-russia-putin-news-03-10-22/h_6dab60cbf1e67184bc76caa599b43fca
Headline: Ukraine continues talks with Russia for evacuation corridors

Description: In Kyiv, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, Minister of Foreign Affairs Dmytro Kuleba had a telephone conversation with Canadian Minister of Foreign Affairs Melanie Joly to discuss ways to stop Russian aggression against Ukraine. They agreed to tighten sanctions pressure on the Russia, Interfax reported. Source link :
Headline: Ukraine and Canada discuss to impose sanctions on Russia

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, in a meeting with the members of the United Kingdom-led Joint Expeditionary Force, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said the country needs to acknowledge it won’t be able to join North Atlantic Treaty Organisation (NATO). Members of the Expeditionary Force include Denmark, Iceland, Latvia, Lithuania, the Netherlands, Norway, Finland, Sweden, and Estonia, TASS reported. Source link :
Headline: Ukrainian President admits they won't be able to join NATO

Description: In Kyiv, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, the General Prosecutor’s Office launched criminal proceedings against 40 Russian nationals, including the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova Aerospace Forces commander Surovikin and CBRN Protection Troops commander Kirillov over encroaching on the territorial integrity of Ukraine, Gazeta Russia reported. Source link : https://www.gazeta.ru/politics/news/2022/03/15/17425669.shtml
Headline: Ukraine launches criminal proceedings against 40 Russian nationals

Description: In Moscow, Moscow Oblast, Russia, as per request lodged by the General Prosecutor’s Office, the communications sector regulator Service for Supervision of Communications, Information Technology and Mass Media (Roskomnadzor) restricted access to 32 online media outlets including Caucasian Knot for reporting on Russian invasion of Ukraine, Svoboda News reported. Source link : https://www.svoboda.org/a/v-rossii-zablokirovan-sayt-izdaniya-kavkazskiy-uzel/31755704.html
Headline: Russian communications sector regulator restricts access to media outlets for reporting on Russia

Description: In Mariupol, Donetsk oblast, Ukraine, Chief of Russia’s National Defence Management Centre Mikhail Mizintsev said that humanitarian corridors from Mariupol will be opened in the eastern and western directions from 10 am on 21 March 2022. He also assured that Russian forcers and units of the Donetsk People’s Republic (DPR) will declare a ceasefire for the given time, TASS reported. Source link :
Headline: Russia open humanitarian corridor in Ukraine's Mariupol

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, the Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy appeals for a meeting with the Russian President Vladimir Putin to end the Ukrainian conflict, Swissinfo reported. Source link : https://www.swissinfo.ch/eng/ukraine-president-zelenskiy-insists-on-meeting-with-putin--interfax-cites-interview/47452200
Headline: Ukrainian president insists on meeting with the Russian President

Description: In Tbilisi, Georgia, Minister of Defence Juansher Burchuladze met the newly-appointed Head of North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) Liaison Office Alexander Vinnikov. They discussed NATO-Georgia relations, as well as the regional security challenges amid the war in Ukraine, Agenda reported. Source link : https://agenda.ge/en/news/2022/845
Headline: Georgian minister meets NATO Liaison Office head

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy urged French companies to stop their operations in Russia after the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Republic World reported. Source link : https://www.republicworld.com/world-news/russia-ukraine-crisis/russia-ukraine-war-news-live-updates-day-28-march-23-2022-liveblog.html
Headline: Ukrainian President urges French firms to stop their operations in Russia

Description: In New Delhi, National Capital Territory of Delhi, India, Foreign Secretary Harsh Vardhan Shringla announced that the government had launched a multi-prong evacuation plan named 'Operation Ganga' to evacuate Indian citizens from Ukraine, amid Russian military invasion of the country. Around 15,000 Indian nationals are believed to be stranded in Ukraine, NDTV reported. Source link : https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/thousands-of-indian-citizens-flown-out-of-ukraine-under-operation-ganga-foreign-secretary-shringla-2793027
Headline: Government launches evacuation plan for Indian nationals stuck in Ukraine

Description: In New Delhi, National Capital Territory of Delhi, India, India urged both Russia and Ukraine to declare a ceasefire in conflict zones amid Russian invasion of Ukraine. There are still 3,000 Indian nationals stuck in eastern cities of Kharkiv, Sumy, and Pisochyn where movement of evacuation buses was not possible due to constant shelling and active military clashes. 10,400 people have been brought back to India in 48 evacuation flights until now, Hindustan Times reported. Source link : https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/india-urges-russia-ukraine-for-a-ceasefire-seeks-to-evacuate-3000-nationals-from-war-zone-101646410694373.html
Headline: India urges ceasefire in Russia-Ukraine conflict

Description: In New Delhi, India, Minister of External Affairs S Jaishankar met Oman's Minister of External Affairs Sayyid Badr Hamad Al Busaidi to strengthen bilateral cooperation. They discussed cooperation in energy, defence, space, science and technology. They also exchanged views on the situation in the Gulf, Yemen, and Ukraine, ANI reported. Source link : https://aninews.in/news/world/asia/india-oman-discuss-cooperation-in-energy-defence-space20220323235335
Headline: India and Oman discuss ways to strengthen bilateral cooperation

Description: In Tehran, Iran, the Iranian Foreign Minister Hossein Amirabdollahian called for an immediate ceasefire between Ukraine and Russia. He further stated claimed that the ongoing crisis was caused by North Atlantic Treaty Organisation (NATO)'s provocations. Al Jazeera reported. Source link :
Headline: Iran calls for a ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine

Description: In Paris, France, the French government and the European Union (EU) urged India to take a stand in support of Ukrainian sovereignty, amid the Russian invasion of the country. EU high representative for Foreign Affairs Josep Borrell discussed the issue with the Indian Minister of External Affairs S Jaishankar, Times of India reported. Source link : https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/indias-key-partner-france-urges-india-to-speak-up-on-ukraine/articleshow/89802572.cms
Headline: France and the EU urge India to take a stand amid the Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In London, England, United Kingdom, the UK barred all Russian-flagged, registered, owned or controlled vessels from its ports, instructing ports to block any such vessels if the ban would be ignored. UK Secretary of Transport Grant Shapps announced the ban under sanctions against Russian Invasion of Ukraine, Politico reported. Source link : https://www.politico.eu/article/uk-bans-russian-ships-from-ports/
Headline: UK bans all Russian vessels from its ports

Description: In London, England, United Kingdom, Belarusian opposition leader Sviatlana Tikhanovskaya met with United Kingdom's Minister of State for Europe and North America James Cleverley to discuss the war in Ukraine and humanitarian response. They also discussed Uk's support to the Belarusian anti-war movement, Twitter account of Sviatlana Tikhanovskaya reported. Source link : https://twitter.com/Tsihanouskaya/status/1501606843122081797
Headline: Belarusian leader meets United Kingdom's minister to discuss war in Ukraine

Description: In Paris, Île-de-France province, France, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs announced that the government allocated USD109 million for humanitarian assistance to Ukraine and the neighboring countries affected by the refugee inflow from Ukraine, Interfax reported. Source link : https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/810361.html
Headline: France allocates humanitarian assistance to Ukraine

Description: In London, England, United Kingdom, the government relaxed its visa issuance procedures for Ukrainian refugees allowing such people to travel to the UK without prior attending a visa processing center. The changes in visa issuance procedures came after the government received criticism for reported delays at application centers, CNN reported. Source link : https://edition.cnn.com/europe/live-news/ukraine-russia-putin-news-03-10-22/h_b05ae7a865fe9d2361556e4c9a42313a
Headline: UK relaxes visa issuance procedures for Ukrainian refugees

Description: At the press conference, Vienna, Austria, International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) did not confirm Russian statements that Belarus had started to supply electricity to the Russian-occupied Chornobyl Nuclear Power Plant in Ukraine, after the facility had been disconnected from Ukraine's power grid, Economic Truth reported. Source link : https://www.epravda.com.ua/rus/news/2022/03/10/683743/
Headline: IAEA denies information on Belarus' electricity supply to the Russian-occupied nuclear power plant

Description: In Brussel, Belgium, the Committee of Ministers of the Council of Europe decided to suspend all relations with Belarus as a result of the country's active participation in Russia's aggression against Ukraine, Interfax reported. Source link : https://interfax.com/newsroom/top-stories/76951/
Headline: Belgium suspends relations with Belarus over Ukraine

Description: In London, England, United Kingdom, the UK Ministry of Defence summoned Russian defence attaché for the second over the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Malay Mail reported. Source link : https://www.malaymail.com/news/world/2022/03/21/uk-summons-russian-defence-attache-over-ukraine-invasion/2048710
Headline: UK government summons Russian official over Ukraine invasion

Description: At the Foreign Affairs Council (FAC), Brussels, Belgium, Estonian Minister of Defence Kalle Laanet said that European Union's support for Ukraine, both financially and diplomatically must continue. He also stated that Estonia supports Ukraine joining the European Union and granting Ukraine the status of a candidate country, BBCM reported. Source link : https://monitoring.bbc.co.uk/product/c203c1je
Headline: Estonia advocates more aid for Ukraine from the European Union

Description: In Ankara, Turkey, Minister of Foreign Affairs Mevlut Cavusoglu met Minister of State, Foreign Affairs, African Integration and Diaspora in Ivory Coast Kandia Camara and said that Turkey will evacuate its citizens in Ukraine on 15 and 16 March 2022. He also said there are evacuation plans been made to rescue nationals from the city of Mariupol, Gazete DuvaR reported. Source link :
Headline: Turkey plans to evacuate citizens from Ukraine

Description: In Moscow, Russia, Iranian Minister of Foreign Affairs Hossein Amir Abdollahian has said that Russia stands by Iran's side in the current talks in Vienna regarding revival of the 2015 nuclear deal of Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA). He also announced that Russia does not see any obstacle in lifting unilateral sanctions on Iran and there is no relation between what is happening in Ukraine and the developments in Vienna talks, BBCM reported. Source link :
Headline: Iran states that Vienna talks are unrelated to Ukraine

Description: In Bratislava city, Bratislava Region, Slovakia, the Ministry of Interior announced that some 37,000 Ukrainian refugees had entered Slovakia since the launch of Russia's invasion of Ukraine, Spectator reported. Source link : https://spectator.sme.sk/c/22851034/news-digest-slovakia-continues-helping-ukraine-and-voices-support-for-its-eu-membership.html
Headline: Slovak government details on the Ukrainian refugees

Description: In Ottawa, Ontario province, Canada, the federal government issued a travel advisory for its nationals against non-essential travel to Belarus as Russia allegedly conducts military exercises in the nation, with a potential risk of an 'armed conflict with Ukraine'. The advisory added that the ability of the Embassy of Canada in Poland to provide consular services in Belarus is extremely limited, Global News reported. Source link : https://globalnews.ca/news/8634579/canada-belarus-travel-advisory/
Headline: Canadian federal government issues travel advisory for its nationals travelling to Belarus

Description: In Kyiv City, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, the Ukrainian Cabinet Ministers approved a draft decree of the President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for Annual National Program under the auspices of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO)-Ukraine Commission for 2022, Pravda reported. Source link : https://www.pravda.com.ua/rus/news/2022/02/23/7325201/
Headline: Ukrainian Cabinet Ministers adopt a draft of Annual National Program for 2022

Description: In Kyiv city, Kyiv, Ukraine, President Volodymyr Zelenskyyi proposed to set up a parliamentary defence coalition authorised to operationally adopt legal and economic decisions related to Ukraine’s defence and security, Hromadske reported. Source link : https://hromadske.ua/posts/u-radi-hochut-stvoriti-oboronnu-koaliciyu-sho-ce-oznachaye
Headline: Ukrainian President propose to set up a parliamentary defence coalition

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, Prime Minister Denis Shmigal announced that the government began establishing four recovery funds, tasked with accumulating funds for the post-Russian invasion reconstruction of the country. The names of the funds are Foundation for the Restoration of Destroyed Property and Infrastructure, Fund for Economic Renewal and Transformation, Debt Service and Repayment Fund, and Fund for the Support of Affected Business, Pravda reported. Source link : https://www.epravda.com.ua/rus/news/2022/03/3/683202/
Headline: Ukraine creates recovery funds for post-war reconstruction

Description: In Tbilisi, Georgia, Ministry of Foreign Affairs announced plans to implement a bus route to transport Georgians from Lyiv, Ukraine to Warsaw, Poland amid the Russian invasion of Ukraine. The report added the bus service will operate between 6 March to 10 March 2022, Agenda reported. Source link : https://agenda.ge/en/news/2022/550
Headline: Georgian government announces plans to implement evacuation route for its nationals in Ukraine

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, Minister of Agrarian Policy and Food Roman Leshchenko announced ban on export of agricultural goods namely wheat, oats, millets, sugar, salt, and alive cattle. The report added that export of certain commodities like meslin, corn, chicken meat, eggs, and sunflower oil will require prior permission from the government, the Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine reported. Source link :
Headline: Ukraine bans export of agricultural goods

Description: In Minsk, Minskaja voblasć, Belarus, the Citizenship and Migration Department of the Belarusian Ministry of Internal Affairs announced it would help displaced people from Ukraine in the event of large flows of refugees leaving the country in case of Russian invasion, Belta reported. Source link : https://eng.belta.by/society/view/migration-official-belarus-is-ready-to-host-migrants-from-ukraine-148043-2022/
Headline: Belarussian government affirms its readiness to accept displaced people from Ukraine

Description: In Kyiv city, Kyiv, Ukraine, Ukraine, the Prime Minister Denys Shmyhal refused to accept Russia’s demands on the conflict resolution. Russia had put forward three requirements: Ukraine’s recognition of the 2014 Crimea annexation, renunciation of Ukraine’s accession plans to North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO), and Ukraine’s partial demilitarisation, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/politics/23/02/2022/621595189a7947f534d5bbf6?from=from_main_7
Headline: Ukrainian PM refuses to accept Russia's conditions

Description: In Warsaw, Poland, Embassy of Thailand laid out a plan to evacuate 230 Thai nationals living in Ukraine amid Russian invasion of the country. The embassy set up a special line group to quickly issue communication and asked for cooperation from the governments of Ukraine and Poland. It stated that Ukraine's western city of Lviv could be used as an evacuation route to Poland, Bangkok Post reported. Source link : https://www.bangkokpost.com/thailand/general/2265859/govt-may-evacuate-thais-in-ukraine-amid-tensions
Headline: Thai government to evacuate nationals from Ukraine

Description: In Tokyo, Japan, Prime Minister Fumio Kishida announced to freeze assets for four more Russian banks, namely, Russia's second-largest bank VTB Bank, Bank Otkritie, Sovcombank and Novikombank, in response to Russia's invasion of Ukraine, Kyodo News reported. Source link :
Headline: Japan freezes assets of four more Russian banks

Description: In Tokyo, Prime Minister Fumio Kishida revoked the ‘most-favoured nation’ (MFN) status of Russia over its invasion of Ukraine and imposed restrictions on the export and import of luxury and other goods. Japan imposed the sanctions in line with Group of Seven (G7) countries, Japan Times reported. Source link :
Headline: Japan revokes Russia's most favoured nation status

Description: In Jerusalem, Israel, Ministry of Foreign Affairs said that Israeli diplomats assisted evacuating of Lebanese, Syrian and Egyptian nationals from Ukraine amid Russian invasion of the country. Nationals of several countries boarded a bus to Polish border organised by Israeli diplomats. The ministry also stated that Israeli embassy in Ukraine is ready to help residents from East Jerusalem, i24NEWS reported. Source link :
Headline: Israel evacuates Lebanese, Syrian and Egyptian nationals from Ukraine

Description: In Washington, D.C., District of Columbia, United States (US), Department of State announced that the government will provide USD54 million to Ukraine in humanitarian aid to help the population deal with the effects of Russian military invasion of the country. The fund would include USD26 million from Department of State and USD28 million from the US Agency for International Development, US Department of State reported. Source link : https://www.state.gov/the-united-states-announces-additional-humanitarian-assistance-for-the-people-of-ukraine/
Headline: US Department of State announces humanitarian aid for Ukraine

Description: At the United Nations, New York, United States, Kenya, Gabon and Ghana voted in favour of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) resolution to condemn Russia’s invasion of Ukraine and demand the immediate withdrawal of troops, United Nations reported. Source link :
Headline: Kenya, Gabon and Ghana vote in favour of the UNSC resolution on Russia’s invasion of Ukraine

Description: In New York, United States, Malawi voted in support of the United Nations (UN) resolution condemning Russian invasion of Ukraine and called for withdrawal of Russian troops, Nyasa Times reported. Source link :
Headline: Malawi votes for UN resolution condemning Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In New York, United States, North Korea voted against United Nations resolution ES-11/1 on withdrawal of Russian forces from Ukraine. 141 countries voted in favor, 34 abstained and 5 voted against the resolution, United Nations reported. Source link :
Headline: North Korea votes against UN resolution on Russia

Description: In New York, United States, Laos abstained from vote on United Nations (UN) resolution ES-11/1 on withdrawal of Russian forces from Ukraine. The Permanent Representative of Laos to the UN Anouparb Vongnorkeo stated that his country remains sceptical of unilateral sanctions and cautioned that such measures could entail long-term impacts on innocent people, especially during the pandemic. 141 countries voted in favor, 34 abstained and 5 voted against the resolution, United Nations reported. Source link :
Headline: Laos abstains from vote on UN resolution on Russia

Description: In the House of Representatives, Washington, District of Columbia, United States, the government approved to ban United States' procurement of Russia-produced crude oil, certain petroleum product, liquified natural gas, and coal, Reuters reported. Source link : https://www.reuters.com/world/us/us-congress-reaches-govt-spending-deal-including-136-bln-ukraine-2022-03-09/
Headline: United States bans procurement of Russian crude oil

Description: At the United Nations Headquarters, New York City, New York state, United States, the UN estimated that 2.8 million Ukrainian residents had fled Ukraine since the beginning of Russia’s military invasion on 24 February 2022, Reuters reported. Source link : https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/russian-attacks-western-ukraine-prompt-more-people-flee-2022-03-14/
Headline: United Nations says 2.8 million Ukrainian residents had fled the conflict

Description: In Washington, District of Colombia, United States, senators from the Republican Party, including John Barrasso, introduced a bill to ban imports of Russian uranium as measures against Russia for its invasion of Ukraine. President Joe Biden had banned imports of oil, natural gas and coal from Russia on 8 March 2022, the Hill reported. Source link :
Headline: US introduces bill to ban Russian uranium imports

Description: In Kyiv city, Kyiv, Ukraine, the Indian embassy issued an advisory for all Indian students to leave Ukraine temporarily, amid reports of potential Russian invasion of Ukraine. The embassy assured that they were working with respective university authorities for streamlining the education process, NDTV reported. Source link : https://www.ndtv.com/indians-abroad/russia-ukraine-crisis-indian-embassy-in-ukraine-again-advises-indian-students-to-return-2782131
Headline: Indian embassy issues advisory to students in Ukraine's Kyiv

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, Pakistani embassy in Ukraine stated that 125 Pakistani nationals were successfully evacuated since Russian invasion of Ukraine. Other 376 nationals have reached the Ukraine-Poland border, four are at the Ukraine-Romania border and nine at the Ukraine-Hungary border, the Tribune Express reported. Source link :
Headline: Pakistan evacuates its citizens from Ukraine

Description: In Buenos Aires, Argentina, Minister of Foreign Affairs Santiago Cafiero confirmed the shutdown of Embassy of Argentina in Ukraine and the evacuation of all the staff amid Russian invasion of the country. Many countries have shifted their embassies from capital city Kyiv to Lviv, the largest city in western part of Ukraine, La Nacion reported. Source link : https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/cierran-la-embajada-argentina-en-kiev-y-evacuan-al-personal-nid03032022/
Headline: Argentinian embassy shuts down operations in Ukraine

Description: At the European Council, Brussels, Belgium, European Council banned any European national or legal entity from participating in transactions with Russia’s central bank or any individual and company, acting on its behalf, Council of the European Union reported. Source link : https://www.consilium.europa.eu/en/press/press-releases/2022/02/28/eu-adopts-new-set-of-measures-to-respond-to-russia-s-military-aggression-against-ukraine/
Headline: European Council imposes sanctions on Russia

Description: At the Europa building, Brussels, Belgium, in a phone conversation with European Council President Charles Michel, Russia’s Vladimir President Putin accused Ukraine of not seriously committing to finding mutually acceptable solutions to the Russia-Ukraine conflict, Pravda reported. Source link : https://www.pravda.com.ua/rus/news/2022/03/15/7331634/
Headline: Russian president accuses Ukraine of not committing to find solutions

Description: At North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) headquarters, Brussels, Belgium, the United States President Joe Biden stated that he 'warned' Chinese President Xi Jinping that taking any steps to aid the Russian invasion of Ukraine would put him and his country in 'significant jeopardy'. He also stated that the US hopes China would understand that its economic future is closely tied to the west than it is to Russia, the Independent reported. Source link : https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/americas/us-politics/biden-russia-ukraine-war-china-xi-jinping-b2043443.html
Headline: US issues China a statement over its engagement with Russia

Description: In Kyiv city, Kyiv, Ukraine, parliament approved amendments to the Budget Code, allowing the government to operationally re-allocate public funds for expenditures of the Armed Forces, Hromadske reported. Source link : https://hromadske.ua/posts/deputati-dozvolili-operativno-vidilyati-koshti-z-derzhbyudzhetu-na-armiyu
Headline: Ukrainian Parliament approves amendments to the Budget Code

Description: In Beirut city, Beirut Governorate, Lebanon, Ministry of Foreign Affairs condemned Russia's invasion of Ukraine and called for an immediate withdrawal of Russian forces from Ukraine, Al Arabiya reported. Source link :
Headline: Lebanese government condemns Russia's invasion of Ukraine

Description: At White House, District of Columbia, United States (US), President Joe Biden signed an executive order on blocking property of certain individuals or legal entities, and banned certain business transactions, related to Ukraine’s separatist republics of Donetsk and Luhansk, White House reported.
