Source link : https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefing-room/presidential-actions/2022/02/21/executive-order-on-blocking-property-of-certain-persons-and-prohibiting-certain-transactions-with-respect-to-continued-russian-efforts-to-undermine-the-sovereignty-and-territorial-integrity-of-ukraine/
Headline: US government signs executive order to block individuals and entities for supporting DPR and LPR separatists

Description: At the Capitol, Washington, D.C., United States, during the State of the Union address, President Joe Biden announced closure of American airspace to all Russian flights. This was in response to Russia's invasion of Ukraine. In North America, US became second country to close its airspace after Canada announced the same on 27 February 2022. Similar restriction was put in place by European Union on 27 February 2022, the White House reported. Source link :
Headline: US President announces closure of American airspace to Russian flights

Description: At the Capitol, Washington, D.C., United States (US), President Joe Biden announced that the government worked with 30 other countries to release 60 million barrels of oil from reserves around the world. The US released 30 million barrels from its own Strategic Petroleum Reserve. The report added that this was done to mitigate rise in energy prices in the country due to sanctions imposed on Russian energy companies such as Gazprom and Rosneft amid Russia's invasion of Ukraine. According to the US Energy Information Administration, Russia was the world’s third-largest producer of petroleum, after the United States and Saudi Arabia in 2020, the White House reported. Source link :
Headline: US President releases oil reserves to mitigate rise in energy prices

Description: In Washington D.C., District of Columbia, United States (US), World Bank suspended all programmes in Russia and Belarus effective immediately due to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, assisted by Belarus. The World Bank Group has not approved any new loans to or investments in Russia since 2014. There has also been no new lending approved to Belarus since mid-2020, World Bank reported. Source link : https://www.worldbank.org/en/news/statement/2022/03/02/world-bank-group-statement-on-russia-and-belarus
Headline: World Bank suspends programmes in Russia and Belarus due to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine

Description: At Verkhovna Rada, Kyiv oblast, Ukraine, the parliament approved a bill in the first reading envisioning to establish a regulatory framework for civilian possession of firearms and ammunition. Implementation of this bill be under Ministry of Internal Affairs, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/society/23/02/2022/62160cca9a79471277144f31?from=newsfeed
Headline: Ukrainian parliament approves a bill to allow for civilian possession of arms and ammunition

Description: At Verkhovna Rada, Kyiv, Ukraine, parliament tabled a draft proposing to close Ukraine’s state border with Russia and Belarus, to introduce martial law in Donetsk and Luhansk regions, and to sever transport connections with Russia, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/politics/23/02/2022/6215ef1c9a79470791fa26f3?from=newsfeed
Headline: Ukrainian government table a draft proposing to close country border with Russia and Belarus

Description: In Moscow, Russian Federation, President Vladimir Putin, during a telephone conversation, with Kazakhstan's President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev informed about his position regarding the situation in Ukraine. The report did not specify the position of Russian President on Ukraine, the President of the Republic of Kazakhstan reported. Source link :
Headline: Russian President informs Kazakhstan's President about his position on Ukraine

Description: In Ankara, Turkey, Minister of Foreign Affairs Mevlut Cavusoglu meets Russian Minister of Foreign Affairs Sergey Viktorovich Lavrov over a phone call. The meeting was held to discuss the latest developments in Ukraine over the Russian invasion, NTV News reported. Source link :
Headline: Turkey's Minister of Foreign Affairs meets Russian Minister of Foreign Affairs to discuss developments in Ukraine

Description: At United Nations Security Council (UNSC), New York, United States, Gabon's Permanent Representative to the United Nations Michel Xavier Biang condemned Russia's deployment of troops in Donetsk Oblast and Luhansk Oblast of Ukraine, the Guardian reported. Source link :
Headline: Gabon condemns Russia's troop deployment in Ukraine

Description: At United Nations Security Council (UNSC), New York, United States, Ghana's Ambassador to United Nations (UN) Harold Agyeman condemned Russia's order of troops deployment in Donetsk Oblast and Luhansk Oblast of Ukraine, the Guardian reported. Source link :
Headline: Ghana condemns Russia's troop deployment in Ukraine

Description: At United Nations Security Council (UNSC), New York, United States, Kenya's Ambassador to United Nations (UN) Martin Kimani condemned Russia's decision to breach territorial integrity of Ukraine by invading it. He also condemned Russia's troop deployment in Donetsk Oblast and Luhansk Oblast of Ukraine, Quartz reported. Source link :
Headline: Kenya condemns Russia's invasion of Ukraine

Description: In United Nations General Assembly (UNGA), New York City New York State, United States (US), the Permanent Representative of Eritrea to the United Nations (UN) Sophia Tesfamariam Yohannes voted against the UN resolution in UNGA against Russia to immediately withdraw its forces from Ukraine. The representative suggested that her country’s experience has demonstrated that all forms of sanctions are counterproductive, United Nations reported. Source link :
Headline: Eritrea votes against UN resolution which calls for Russia to immediately withdraw its forces from Ukraine

Description: At the United Nations Building, New York City, United States, United Nations Secretary General António Guterres announced that the UN would allocate USD40 million from the central emergency. This fund was in response to intensify vital assistance and reach the most vulnerable, as millions of people in Ukraine faced hunger and shortage of water and medicine supplies, Al Wasat reported. Source link :
Headline: UN Secretary General announces aid to Ukraine people

Description: In Kremlin, Moscow city, Moskva Oblast, Russian Federation, the President Vladimir Putin announced that Russia would treat any country, enforcing a non-fly zone over Ukraine as a direct participant in the war. Earlier, the Ukrainian President Volodomyr Zelensky called on the United States for assistance in establishing a no-fly zone over Ukraine, CNN reported. Source link : https://edition.cnn.com/europe/live-news/ukraine-russia-putin-news-03-05-22/h_6ecbb18224dc7ef55170557a28047547
Headline: Russian President warns countries against enforcing a no-fly zone over Ukraine

Description: In Kremlin, Moskva oblast, Russian Federation, the Russian Minister of Foreign Affairs Sergey Lavrov agreed to negotiate with Ukraine, if the President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's government falls first, La Politica reported. Source link : https://www.lapoliticaonline.com/espana/internacionales-es/rusia-condiciona-el-dialogo-con-ucrania-a-la-caida-de-zelenski/
Headline: Russian official announces dialogue with Ukraine conditions in Russia's Moskva oblast

Description: In New Aquitaine region, France, regional councillors Alain Rousset and Isabelle Boudineau said that the region was to donate USD300,000 to Ukraine as a measure to support local Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs) in light of Russian invasion of Ukraine, Sud Ouest reported. Source link : https://www.sudouest.fr/international/europe/ukraine/region-nouvelle-aquitaine-300-000-euros-en-faveur-de-l-ukraine-9558860.php
Headline: France's New Aquitaine region donates to NGOs supporting Ukraine

Description: In Mariupol, Donetsk region, Ukraine, Armed Forces of Ukraine managed to evacuate some 400 residents from the city, sieged by Russian Armed Forces troops, Pravda reported. Source link : https://www.pravda.com.ua/rus/news/2022/03/6/7328714/
Headline: Ukraine evacuates 400 residents from Mariupol

Description: In Washington, District of Columbia, United States, the federal government announced that they plan to accept up to 100,000 Ukrainians fleeing Russia's invasion and pledged USD1 billion in additional humanitarian aid. The report added that more than 3.5 million people have fled since Russia invaded Ukraine on 24 February to neighbouring European countries, Channel News Asia reported. Source link : https://www.channelnewsasia.com/world/us-accept-100000-ukrainians-fleeing-war-amid-broader-aid-effort-2583811
Headline: US plans to accept Ukrainians fleeing Russia's invasion

Description: In Madrid, Spain, Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez announced the authorities had temporarily seized a Russian oligarch's yacht worth USD140 million in Barcelona. The yacht was owned by the head of Russian state conglomerate Rostec, Sergei Chemezov. Russia was sanctioned by several countries for invading Ukraine, USNews reported. Source link :
Headline: Spain temporarily seizes Russian oligarch's yacht

Description: In Tallinn, Harju maakond, Estonia, Premier Kaja Kallas stated that Russian President Vladimir Putin was “enjoying the international attention generated by Russia’s military drills” and that no concession of any kind must be given to Russia as long as it maintained its troops near the border with Ukraine, NDTV reported. Source link :
Headline: Estonian Prime Minister suggests to not give any concessions to Russia

Description: In Bratislava, Slovakia, the Ministry of the Interior stated that so far 79,059 foreign nationals had entered Slovakia since the launch of Russia’s military invasion of Ukraine, adding that most of them were Ukrainian refugees, Spectator reported. Source link : https://spectator.sme.sk/c/22853807/news-digest-people-to-rally-in-support-of-ukraine-hear-zelensky-speak-live.html
Headline: Slovak Ministry of Interior reports 79,059 foreign nationals had entered Slovakia

Description: In Warsaw, Poland, Prime Minister Mateusz Morawiecki announced to seize properties and assets of Russian nationals in response to the Russian invasion of Ukraine. He said that there were constitutional restrictions on such actions, but the government will implore options, TASS reported. Source link :
Headline: Poland plans to seize Russian's properties and assets in the country

Description: In Tokyo, Japan, the government imposed sanctions by freezing assets of 17 Russian nationals amid Russian invasion of Ukraine. The sanctioned Russians include billionaire Viktor Vekselberg, 11 members of the Duma (legislative assembly), and five family members of banker Yuri Kovalchuk. The additional sanctions bring the total number of Russian nationals targeted by Japan to 61, the Japan Times reported. Source link :
Headline: Japan sanctions 17 Russian nationals

Description: In Ottawa, Ontario, Canada, the government imposed new sanctions on Russia, pursuant to Special Economic Measures (Russia) Regulations and the Special Economic Measures (Ukraine) Regulations imposed dealings prohibitions on listed persons. These measures prohibited persons in Canada and Canadians outside Canada from any engagement related to any property of these listed persons or provision of financial or related services to them. The new amendments impose restrictions on 31 individuals who are key members of President Putin’s inner circle and 27 key financial institutions including Public Joint Stock Company Sberbank of Russia (Sberbank) and VTB Bank Public Joint Stock Company (VTB). In addition, the amendments will include 351 members of the Russian State Duma who voted for the decision to recognize the independence of the so-called Donetsk People's Republic (DPR) and Luhansk People's Republic (LPR), Government of Canada reported. Source link :
Headline: Canada imposes sanctions in response to Russia's invasion of Ukraine

Description: In Washington, United States, Secretary of State Antony Blinken said that the US is providing over USD186 million as additional humanitarian assistance to Ukrainians internally displaced or forced to flee abroad after Russian invasion of Ukraine. He also stated that the United States is the largest single-country donor of humanitarian assistance to Ukraine, as it has already provided USD293 million since the start of the Russian invasion, Axios reported. Source link :
Headline: United States provides additional humanitarian aid to Ukraine

Description: In Moscow, Russia, Deputy Head of Sudan’s ruling Sovereign Council Mohamed Hamdan Daglo arrived in the city to support Russian President Vladimir Putin’s decision to recognise the separatist regions of Donetsk and Luhansk in eastern Ukraine and to deploy troops in the region, Sudan Tribune reported. Source link :
Headline: Sudan supports Russian recognition of Ukraine's separatist regions

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, National Security and Defence Council (NSDC) secretary Oleksiy Danilov cited intelligence, saying that Russia was planning to introduce a nationwide martial law on 4 March 2022, Ukrinform reported. Source link : https://www.ukrinform.net/rubric-ato/3418415-on-march-4-russia-set-to-impose-martial-law-ukraines-nsdc-secretary.html
Headline: Ukraine says Russia was planning to introduce a nationwide martial law

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, the advisor to the Ukrainian President's Chief of Staff, Oleksiy Arestovich said the war will likely end by early May after Russia runs out of resources to attack its neighbour and the exact timing would depend on how much resources it was willing to commit, Unian reported. Source link :
Headline: Ukrainian Presidential Advisor says war will likely end by early May

Description: In Moscow, Russia, Iranian Minister of Foreign Affairs Hossein Amir-Abdollahian said Russia was not creating obstacles for the Iranian Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA) nuclear deal negotiations, and the delay in talks was not related to the situation in Ukraine. Earlier, European Union High Representative for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy Josep Borrell said that the negotiations were paused due to “external factors”, TASS reported. Source link :
Headline: Iran says Russia was not hindering nuclear talks

Description: In Kyiv, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, the parliament approved President Volodymyr Zelenskyy proposal to extend the martial law regime for 30 more days until 25 April 2022. The martial law was imposed on 24 February 2022 after the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Forbes reported. Source link : https://www.forbes.ru/society/459115-verhovnaa-rada-ukrainy-prodlila-voennoe-polozenie-v-strane-ese-na-30-dnej?utm_source=facebook&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=verhovnaya-rada-ukrainy-prinyala-reshenie-p
Headline: Ukrainian Parliament extends the martial law regime

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy signed a decree to combine all national TV channels into one platform. He said it was important to have a ‘unified national policy’ under martial law, Reuters reported. Source link :
Headline: President combines all TV channels in Ukraine

Description: In London, England, United Kingdom, Secretary of Foreign Affairs Lizz Truss called upon China to pressure Russia to de-escalate the tensions with Ukraine. She stated that China’s support to Russia was inconsistent with the country’s non-interference policy, Reuters reported. Source link :
Headline: United Kingdom urges China to pressure Russia

Description: In Strasbourg, Grand Est, France, Ukrainian Prime Minister Denys Shmyhal urged the Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe (PACE) to expel Russia from the Council of Europe (COE) and said, “it had no right to remain a member of the pan-European rights body after invading its neighbour”. The committee of ministers had suspended Russia’s rights of representation a day after it invaded Ukraine, France24 reported. Source link :
Headline: Ukraine urges Council of Europe to expel Russia

Description: In London, United Kingdom, Deputy Prime Minister Dominic Raab said the UK supported an International Criminal Court (ICC) investigation into allegations of war crimes in Ukraine and it should be offered extra support by member countries. A government statement had earlier announced it was offering police and military analysis, and specialist IT help to collect and preserve evidence for the ICC, Channel News Asia reported. Source link :
Headline: UK voices support for ICC's inquiry into war crimes

Description: In London, England, United Kingdom, Chancellor of the Exchequer Rishi Sunak said that the sanctions imposed on Russia for invading Ukraine will add to the cost-of-living crisis ahead of energy bills soaring on 1 April 2022. The government had imposed large import tariff hikes on Russian products, Sky News reported. Source link :
Headline: UK Chancellor says Russian sanctions will add to cost-of-living

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy imposed a martial law across the country against Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, BBC reported. Source link : https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-60503037
Headline: Ukrainian President imposes martial law across the country

Description: In Minsk city, Minskaja voblasć, Belarus, the Ukraine and Russia concluded the first round of bilateral negotiations aimed at agreeing a ceasefire in Ukraine. Both sides failed to reach an agreement. Ukraine demanded an immediate ceasefire and withdrawal of Russian troops, with the two sides agreeing to a second round of talks in the coming days, CBC News reported. Source link : https://www.cbsnews.com/live-updates/russia-ukraine-news-kyiv-war-putin-invasion-talks-today/
Headline: Ukraine and Russia conclude the first round of bilateral negotiations in Belarus's Minskaja voblasć

Description: In Tokyo, Japan, Fumio Kishida stated the government will first accept individuals who have fled Ukraine and have relatives or acquaintances in Japan. He stated even individuals without any relatives will be allowed and there will be no daily cap on the number of arrivals, Kyodo News reported. Source link :
Headline: Japan accepts Ukrainian refugees fleeing conflict

Description: In Tokyo, Japan, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs banned its nationals from traveling to areas in Russia and Belarus that border Ukraine and issued a Level 4 travel alert. Level 4 is the highest in the four-tier system, NHK World Japan reported. Source link :
Headline: Japan bans travelling to areas in Russia and Belarus

Description:
In Jerusalem, Israel, Minister of Foreign Affairs Yair Lapid announced that the government has helped over 4,000 Israeli nationals stuck in Ukraine amid Russian invasion of the country. On 1 March 2022,  a repatriation flight from Romania landed at Ben Gurion airport is Israel's Tel Aviv. The evacuated nationals included members of the Arab minority, Times of Israel reported. 
 Source link :
Headline: Israel evacuates its citizens from Ukraine

Description: In New York, United States, at the 76th session of the United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) on Ukraine, China's Permanent Representative to the United Nations Zhang Jun urged all parties to exercise restraint and de-escalate the situation in Ukraine, Firstpost reported. Source link :
Headline: China urges restraint and de-escalation amid tensions in Ukraine

Description: In Washington, District of Columbia, United States, the Department of the Treasury’s Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) imposed expansive economic sanctions as a response against Russia's further invasion of Ukraine. This would have a long term implications which specifically target the core structure of the Russian financial system. The actions also target nearly 80 percent of all banking assets in Russia including two of its largest bank, Public Joint Stock Company Sberbank of Russia (Sberbank) and VTB Bank Public Joint Stock Company (VTB Bank). The OFAC also imposed blocking sanctions on three additional major Russian financial institutions. These are Public Joint Stock Company Bank Financial Corporation (Otkritie), Open Joint Stock Company Sovcombank (Sovcom), Joint Stock Commercial Bank Novikombank (Novikom). Additionally, 12 Otkritie subsidiaries and 22 Sovcombank subsidiaries in Russia and Cyprus were also blocked, US Department of Treasury reported. Source link :
Headline: US Department of Treasury announces economic sanctions against Russia

Description: In Washington, District of Columbia, United States, the US Department of the Treasury’s Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned 24 Belarusian individuals and entities due to the country's support for Russian invasion of Ukraine. The US targeted two Government of Belarus (GoB) owned banks under sanctions. These are Belarussian Bank of Development and Reconstruction Belinvestbank Joint Stock Company (Belinvestbank) and Bank Dabrabyt Joint-Stock Company (Bank Dabrabyt). The OFAC also decided to take action against the defense and related materiel sector of the Belarus economy. This included Minsk Wheeled Tractor Plant along with two of its senior executives Aliaksei Ivanavich Rymasheuski (Rymasheuski) and Aliaksandr Piatrovich Vetsianevich (Vetsianevich), US Department of the Treasury reported. Source link :
Headline: US Department of Treasury sanctions Belarusian individuals and entities for their support to Russia

Description: In Washington D.C, Department of State spokesperson Ned Price said that National Security Adviser Jake Sullivan cautioned China’s Office of the Foreign Affairs Commission Yang Jiechi against helping Russia in Ukraine and threatened consequences if it provided support to Russia, Al Jazeera reported. Source link :
Headline: US warns China against helping Russia

Description: In Washington, District of Colombia, United States, White House press secretary Jen Psaki said that President Joe Biden will travel to Europe next week for face-to-face talks with North Atlantic Treaty Organisation (NATO) and the European Union about further sanctions on Russia and humanitarian efforts for Ukraine, Al Jazeera reported. Source link :
Headline: US President announces to attend NATO, EU summits

Description: In Washington, District of Columbia, United States, chief of the Department of Justice task force targeting assets of Russian oligarchs, Andrew Adams announced to have seen evidence of attempts to evade the sanctions imposed after the invasion of Ukraine or to move assets ahead of possible sanctions, Jerusalem Post reported. Source link : https://www.jpost.com/international/article-702991
Headline: US task force on Russian oligarchs sees evidence of sanctions evasion

Description: In Moscow, Russia, Ministry of Defence announced ceasefire and humanitarian corridors out of Ukrainian cities of Kyiv, Kharkiv, Sumy, and Mariupol on 7 March 2022 amid Russian invasion of Ukraine. The Kyiv and Kharkiv corridors would lead to Belarusian or Russian territory. The move came after two failed attempts on 5-6 March 2022 to evacuate civilians out of besieged city of Mariupol, Reuters reported. Source link : https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/russia-open-humanitarian-corridors-march-7-interfax-2022-03-07/
Headline: Russian Ministry of Defence announces ceasefire and humanitarian corridors  in Ukraine

Description: In Brussels, Belgium, the European Commission signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) for USD1.3 billion to provide macro-financial assistance (MFA) to Ukraine amid Russian invasion of the country. The fund will help Ukraine to finance its urgent needs and will be made available in the form of long-term loans on highly favourable terms, European Commission reported. Source link :
Headline: European Commission finalises financial assistance package to Ukraine amid Russian invasion

Description: In Washington D.C, United States, Secretary of State, Antony Blinken spoke with Group of Seven (G7) Foreign Ministers of Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, the United Kingdom, and the High Representative of the European Union. They were joined by Foreign Minister of Ukraine Dmytro Kuleba. They reaffirmed their support to Ukraine's sovereignty and territorial integrity. They discussed shared responses to Russian invasion of Ukraine and indicated that all these countries imposed several costs and consequences to hold Russia accountable, the US Department of State reported. Source link : https://www.state.gov/secretary-blinkens-call-with-g7-foreign-ministers-and-ukraine-foreign-minister-kuleba/
Headline: G7 countries reaffirm their support to Ukraine

Description: At the United Nations, New York City, United States, United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) adopted a resolution demanding the Russia immediately end its invasion of Ukraine and withdraw all its military forces from the country. The resolution was adopted after 141 member states voted in favour, 35 abstained, and 5 voted against it. Belarus, North Korea, Eritrea, Russia, and Syria were countries who voted against the resolution. The report added that this was UNGA's 11th emergency special session since its foundation in 1945. The session opened on 28 February 2022, following the failure of UN Security Council to adopt a resolution condemning the Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, the United Nations reported Source link :
Headline: UN General Assembly adopts a resolution demanding immediate withdrawal of Russian troops in Ukraine

Description: In Washington, District of Columbia, United States, the Department of the Treasury’s Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned 328 members of the Russian State Duma, and the head of Sberbank, Herman Gref. This is fifth package of sanctions imposed on Russian individuals and entities by US since Russia's invasion of Ukraine began on 24 February, US Department of the Treasury reported. Source link : https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2022/03/24/us-sanctions-russia-lawmakers-defense-contractors-a77070
Headline: US sanctions members of the Russian State Duma

Description: In Moscow city, Moskva Oblast, Russian Federation, the Russia’s President Putin formalised the ratification of Russia’s treaty on friendship, cooperation and assistance with Ukraine’s Donetsk People's Republic (DPR) and Luhansk People's Republic (LPR) separatist, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/politics/22/02/2022/6214f38c9a7947bf83709301?from=newsfeed
Headline: Russian President ratified cooperation with DPR and LPR separatist republics

Description: In Kherson, Ukraine, the municipal authorities claimed that Russia had refused to open a humanitarian corridor to supply foodstuff and blocked cargo vehicles with humanitarian aid at the approach to the city, Korrespondent reported. Source link : https://korrespondent.net/ukraine/4454143-rossyia-ne-razreshyla-postavky-humpomoschy-v-kherson
Headline: Ukraine claims Russia refused to open a humanitarian corridor

Description: In Washington D.C., District of Columbia, United States, Apple halted the sales of iPhones and all other products in Russia and limited the provision of Apple Pay and other similar services due to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, CNET News reported. Source link : https://www.cnet.com/news/apple-stops-selling-products-in-russia-limits-app-store-apple-pay-and-maps/
Headline: Apple stop the sales of all products in Russia

Description: In Washington D.C., District of Columbia, United States, US ExxonMobil announced the suspension of its operations at the oil and gas production project Sakhalin-1, adding that it would not conduct any new investments in Russia due to Russian invasion of Ukraine, ExxonMobil reported. Source link : https://corporate.exxonmobil.com/News/Newsroom/News-releases/2022/0301_ExxonMobil-to-discontinue-operations-at-Sakhalin-1_make-no-new-investments-in-Russia
Headline: US ExxonMobil suspends its operations in Russia

Description: In Moscow, Russia, the government is considering nationalising the properties of over 400 international companies that suspended their corporate activities in Russia amid the invasion of Ukraine. The government is monitoring foreign companies for compliance with labor contracts and protection of workers' rights, DW reported. Source link : https://www.dw.com/ru/k-chemu-privedet-nacionalizacija-inostrannyh-kompanij-v-rossii/a-61173620?maca=rus-facebook-dw&fbclid=IwAR347AKhEZkWOVuY6nvDeDnsHLPvOsIFX9DJRpS2J0r3YwLs6hCob20aAvA
Headline: Russia considers nationalising properties of foreign companies

Description: In London, England, United Kingdom (UK), government banned UK nationals and legal entities from participating in transactions that involve Central Bank of the Russian Federation (CBR), Russian National Wealth Fund, and Russia's Ministry of Finance. The decision came in response to Russian invasion of Ukraine, Government of UK reported. Source link : https://www.gov.uk/government/news/uk-statement-on-further-economic-sanctions-targeted-at-the-central-bank-of-the-russian-federation
Headline: UK government announces additional sanctions on Russia

Description: In Berlin, Germany, Federal Minister of Foreign Affairs Annalena Baerbock urged for European peace order to be preserved in light of Russia's invasion of Ukraine, announcing that the government held talks with Eastern European countries to jointly facilitate the accommodation of war refugees from Ukraine, WELT reported. Source link : https://www.welt.de/politik/ausland/article237203069/Baerbock-ueber-Ukraine-Fluechtlinge-Wir-werden-alle-aufnehmen.html
Headline: Germany hold talks on accommodation of war refugees from Ukraine

Description: At the International Monetary Fund (IMF), Washington D.C., District of Columbia, United States, the IMF scheduled a meeting for later this week to review the Ukrainian government's request for emergency macro-financial assistance via the Rapid Financing Instrument, Ukrinform reported. Source link : https://www.ukrinform.net/rubric-ato/3417538-imf-world-bank-preparing-3b-support-package-for-ukraine.html
Headline: IMF reviews Ukrainian government request for emergency financial assistance

Description: In Ottawa, Ontario, Canada, the government barred all Russian-flagged, registered, owned or controlled vessels from entering Canadian seaports and internal waters. The move came in response to Russian invasion of Ukraine. Canada is also tracking three Russian-flagged vessels off the East Coast, Reuters reported. Source link : https://www.reuters.com/world/canada-shuts-ports-russian-ships-widening-sanctions-over-ukraine-invasion-2022-03-01/
Headline: Canada bans Russian ships from entering its ports

Description: In Washington D.C., District of Columbia, United States, the World Bank Group developed a USD3 billion emergency package for Ukraine and scheduled to approve the first instalment of USD350 million later this week, to be followed by a second tranche of USD200 million, Ukrinform reported. Source link : https://www.ukrinform.net/rubric-ato/3417538-imf-world-bank-preparing-3b-support-package-for-ukraine.html
Headline: World Bank Group develops emergency package for Ukraine

Description: In Kyiv, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy signed a bill on the compulsory confiscation of Ukraine-based real estate assets, owned by Russian nationals or Russian entities. This move comes amid Russian invasion of Ukraine. It was passed in the Parliament on 3 March 2022 and would allow Ukraine to confiscate any Russian property in their country without any compensation, the Free Press Journal reported. Source link : https://nv.ua/ukraine/politics/konfiskaciya-rossiyskogo-imushchestva-v-ukraine-zelenskiy-podpisal-zakon-50223838.html
Headline: Ukrainian President signs bill on Russian real estate assets

Description: In Berlin, Germany, Federal Minister of Foreign Affairs Annalena Baerbock announced that the government would provide Ukraine with additional humanitarian aid worth EUR16 million, WELT reported. Source link : https://www.welt.de/politik/ausland/article237203069/Baerbock-ueber-Ukraine-Fluechtlinge-Wir-werden-alle-aufnehmen.html
Headline: Germany announces humanitarian aid for UIkraine

Description: In London, England, United Kingdom, UK imposed a raft of sanctions against Belarusian organisations and individuals including military manufacturers and military aircraft repair plants. This was in response to Belarus' role in Russian invasion of Ukraine. Those sanctioned included Belarus Chief of the General Staff and First Deputy Minister of Defence Major General Victor Gulevich, Deputy Minister of Defence Major General Andrey Zhuk, and Deputy Minister of Defence for Armament and Chief of Armament of the Belarusian Armed Forces Major General Sergei Simonenko. JSC 558 Aircraft Repair Plant and JSC Integral, a military semi-conductor manufacturer were also sanctioned, Government of United Kingdom reported. Source link : https://www.gov.uk/government/news/uk-imposes-sanctions-on-belarus-for-its-role-in-the-russian-invasion-of-ukraine
Headline: UK imposes sanctions on Belarusian organisations and individuals

Description: In Washington, D.C., District of Columbia, United States (US), the government announced additional economic sanctions on Russia for its invasion of Ukraine. They include blocking of 22 Russian defence-related entities, restriction of export of oil and gas extraction equipment, ban on entities supporting Russia’s and Belarus’s military, extension of export control policies on Belarus, and ban on Russian aircraft from entering and using US airspace, the White House reported. Source link : https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefing-room/statements-releases/2022/03/02/fact-sheet-the-united-states-continues-to-impose-costs-on-russia-and-belarus-for-putins-war-of-choice/
Headline: US government announces additional sanctions on Russia

Description: In Vienna, Austria, Raiffeisen Bank International (RBI) announced that it was considering a possible 'carefully managed' withdrawal from the Russian market amid Russian invasion of Ukraine. It briefed on alternative strategic options for its branch operation in Russia, Raiffeisen Bank International reported. Source link : https://www.rbinternational.com/en/media/2022/rbi-is-assessing-all-strategic-options-for-the-future-of-raiffeisenbank-russia.html
Headline: Austria’s RBI bank considers withdrawal from Russian market

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, the Embassy of Argentina advised its nationals residing in  the country to leave amid Russian invasion of Ukraine, Ambito reported. Source link : https://www.ambito.com/politica/ucrania/el-gobierno-pide-los-argentinos-que-se-encuentren-que-abandonen-el-territorio-n5379718
Headline: Argentine Embassy issues advisory to its nationals to leave Ukraine

Description: In Bratislava, Slovakia, Minister of Foreign Affairs Ivan Korčok announced that the Slovak Embassy in Kyiv would remain open despite the Russia's invasion of Ukraine, adding that the embassy building currently sheltered 19 people, Spectator reported. Source link : https://spectator.sme.sk/c/22851798/news-digest-no-need-to-purchase-iodine-pills-or-arrange-passports-authorities-calm-people-down.html
Headline: Slovak government keeps its embassy in Ukraine open

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, Brazilian Ambassador to Ukraine Norton de Andrade Mello Rapesta said that he and his team had moved to Lviv for security reasons after Russian invasion of Ukraine, G1 reported. Source link : https://g1.globo.com/mundo/ucrania-russia/noticia/2022/03/01/por-razoes-de-seguranca-embaixador-brasileiro-na-ucrania-e-equipe-deixam-a-capital-kiev.ghtml
Headline: Brazilian ambassador to Ukraine relocates for security reasons

Description: In Kyiv, Kyiv oblast, Ukraine, Prime Minister Denis Shmyhal stated that Russian invasion of Ukraine had so far inflicted over USD565 billion of financial losses on the country's economy, Pravda Reported. Source link : https://www.epravda.com.ua/news/2022/03/16/684108/
Headline: Ukrainian Prime Minister briefs over financial losses after Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In Quito, Ecuador, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs announced that approximately 1,500 Ecuadorians were identified in risk zones in Ukraine due to Russia's military invasion of Ukraine and that it would activate a planned protocol with the Red Cross only when there would be guarantees for an evacuation, Ecuavisa reported. Source link : https://www.ecuavisa.com/noticias/ecuador/alrededor-de-1500-ecuatorianos-estan-en-riesgo-en-ucrania-no-hay-condiciones-para-activar-corredor-humanitario-DE1372621
Headline: Ecuadorian Ministry of Foreign Affairs identify its citizens stranded in risk zones in Ukraine

Description: In Buenos Aires, Argentina, the governors of Mendoza, Corrientes, Jujuy and the City of Buenos Aires said that they were open to taking refugees in from Ukraine, Los Andes reported. Source link : https://www.losandes.com.ar/politica/suarez-y-los-gobernadores-de-jxc-quieren-recibir-a-refugiados-ucranianos
Headline: Argentine Governors express plans to take refugees from Ukraine

Description: In Kyiv, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced that the government was to abolish most business licenses and permits, to launch corporate loans with zero interest rate, and to insure bank deposits of individuals for the duration of the martial law. He said that the government has already prepared a decision to expand the lending program for entrepreneurs, DW reported Source link : https://www.dw.com/uk/ukraina-hotuie-biznes-kredyty-pid-nul-vidsotkiv-i-harantiiu-100-vidsotkiv-depozytu/a-61168299?maca=ukr-Facebook-dw&fbclid=IwAR2tmTi-7u8cVLRZ4UN8b2u1UBfaLgnH7BENSAtsj9BJO7SHB_qQNyPP6nE
Headline: President announces changes in business programs in Ukraine's Kyiv

Description: In Warsaw, Mazowieckie, Poland, the Ministry of Foreign Affair warned its civilians against any travel to north-eastern, south-eastern and eastern Ukraine and told any Polish nationals living there to leave, the First News reported. Source link : https://www.thefirstnews.com/article/polands-foreign-ministry-advises-against-travel-to-ukraine-27996
Headline: Poland's Ministry of Foreign Affair warns against travel to Ukraine

Description: At the Congress of the Conservative Party, Boston, England, United Kingdom, Prime Minister Borris Johnson warned against seeking political rapprochement with Russian President Vladimir Putin after Russian invasion in Ukraine. He also emphasized on not repeating the 2014 mistake made after Russia's annexation of Crimea, Kommersant reported. Source link : https://www.kommersant.ru/doc/5269472
Headline: United Kingdom warns against the normalization of relations with Russia

Description: In Quito, Ecuador, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs confirmed the first humanitarian flight. It enabled to transfer Ecuadorian citizens who were in Ukraine from Poland on 28 February 2022, amid Russia's invasion of Ukraine, El Comercio reported. Source link : https://www.elcomercio.com/actualidad/politica/cancilleria-confirma-vuelo-humanitario-ecuatorianos-ucrania.html
Headline: Ecuadorian Foreign Ministry confirms first humanitarian flight from Poland amid Russia's invasion of Ukraine

Description: In Quito, Ukraine, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs reported that 319 Ecuadorians left Ukraine as a result of the Russian military invasion and were hosted in five different countries such as Poland, Hungary, Slovakia, Moldova and Romania, Expreso reported. Source link : https://www.expreso.ec/actualidad/cinco-paises-han-recibido-319-ecuatorianos-salieron-ucrania-122575.html
Headline: Ecuadorian Foreign Ministry confirms its nationals are hosted in other countries due to Russia's invasion of Ukraine

Description: At the ICC, The Hague, Zuid-Holland, Netherlands, International Criminal Court (ICC) scheduled the first public hearing to review Ukraine’s allegation of genocide against Russia. The complaint lodged against Russia is scheduled for 7 March-8 March 2022, Barrons reported. Source link : https://www.barrons.com/news/icj-to-hold-ukraine-war-hearings-on-march-7-8-court-01646161208
Headline: ICC schedules the first public hearing to review Ukraine’s allegation of genocide

Description: In Kyiv, Kyiv oblast, Ukraine, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy signed a bill criminalising acts of cooperation with Russia and the Russian public administrations set up in the occupied Ukrainian territories of Donetsk and Luhansk, Pravda reported. Source link : https://www.pravda.com.ua/news/2022/03/15/7331528/
Headline: Ukrainian President signs a bill criminalising acts of cooperation with Russia

Description: In Mariupol, Donetsk region, Ukraine, Deputy Chairman of the humanitarian operation Kyrylo Tymoshenko said that some 20,000 civilians managed to individually evacuate from the war-affected city via a humanitarian corridor negotiated with Russia’s armed forces, Pravda reported. Source link : https://www.pravda.com.ua/news/2022/03/15/7331654/
Headline: Official says 20,000 civilians used the humanitarian corridor to flee war

Description: In Kyiv city, Kyiv, Ukraine, Russian Ministry of Foreign Affairs commenced an evacuation of all staff members working in Russia’s embassy in Ukraine’s capital of Kyiv and its consulates in the cities of Kharkiv, Odesa and Lviv, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/politics/23/02/2022/62161a5e9a79471495d8ddb3?from=newsfeed
Headline: Russian Ministry of Foreign Affairs commences an evacuation of all staff members working in Ukraine

Description: In Moscow city, Moskova, Russian Federation, Head of Ministry of Emergency Situations Alexander Chupriyan estimated that in the event of a further Donbas conflict escalation, the number of refugees from Ukraine’s Donetsk People's Republic (DPR) and Luhansk People's Republic (LPR) occupied territories to Russia could scale up to 500,000 civilians, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/rbcfreenews/6215f02b9a7947084d306533?from=newsfeed
Headline: Russian Ministry briefs on the refugee crisis due to escalation of conflict

Description: In Ankara, Turkey, Minister of Foreign Affairs Mevlut Cavusoglu informed that no country had yet requested a permission to pass warships through the Turkey-controlled Dardanelles and Bosphorus straits on the Black Sea since the start of Russian invasion in Ukraine. Under the 1936 Montreux Convention, Turkey has control over the two straits that connect the Mediterranean and Black Sea, and can limit the passage of warships during wartime or if threatened, Aljazeera reported. Source link : https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/3/1/turkey-blocks-warships-from-straits-amid-russia-ukraine-crisis
Headline: Turkish minister briefs on passage of warships

Description: At the ICC, The Hague, Zuid-Holland Province, Netherlands, the Prosecutor Office of the International Criminal Court (ICC) Karim Khan announced plans to request an ICC approval to launch investigations against Russia. They will be investigating possible war crimes committed in Ukraine, Reuters reported. Source link : https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/icc-prosecutor-proceed-with-investigation-into-alleged-war-crimes-ukraine-2022-02-28/
Headline: Netherlands Prosecutor Office of the ICC briefs possible war crimes against Russia

Description: In Moscow city, Moskova, Russian Federation, President Vladimir Putin ordered to launch a special military operation in Ukraine’s Donbas conflict zone claiming its objective to be the provision of assistance to the Ukrainian civilian population, subjected to several-year genocide, BBC reported. Source link : https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-60503037
Headline: Russian President order to launch a special military operation in Ukraine’s Donbas conflict zone

Description: In Antalya, Turkey, at negotiations mediated by Turkey, Russia’s Minister of Foreign Affairs Sergei Lavrov and Ukraine’s Minister of Foreign Affairs Dmytro Kuleba failed to agree on a ceasefire, humanitarian corridors, or any other ways to reduce the scope or impact of hostilities related to Russia’s military invasion of Ukraine, Reuters reported. Source link : https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/ukraine-accuses-russia-genocide-after-bombing-childrens-hospital-2022-03-10/
Headline: Russia and Ukraine fail to agree on a ceasefire

Description: In Istanbul, Turkey, Minister of Foreign Affairs Mevlut Cavusoglu stated that Russia and Ukraine’s Minister of Foreign Affairs had almost reached an agreement on critical issues and added that this was likely to result in a ceasefire regime in Ukraine, Reuters reported. Source link : https://www.reuters.com/world/turkey-says-russia-ukraine-getting-closer-agreement-critical-issues-2022-03-20/
Headline: Turkey states that Russia and Ukraine are nearing agreement on critical issues

Description: In Washington, District of Columbia, United States, Secretary of State Antony Blinken suspended operations at the US Embassy in Belarus and authorised the departure of non-essential staff from US Embassy in Moscow, after Russian invasion of Ukraine, Radio Free Europe reported. Source link : https://www.rferl.org/a/31728309.html
Headline: United States suspends operations at its embassy in Belarus after Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, President Volodymyr Zelensky announced to severe diplomatic relations with Russia in response to Russia’s military invasion in Ukraine. He had received a request from the Ukrainian Ministry of Foreign Affairs to break the diplomatic relations, Interfax reported. Source link : https://www.interfax.ru/world/824153
Headline: Ukraine severs diplomatic relations with Russia

Description: In Bishkek, Chuy Province, Kyrgyzstan, Kyrgyz Ambassador to Ukraine Idris Kadyrkulov announced that authorities had evacuated 112 Kyrgyz nationals, including 2 embassy staff. They were evacuated from Ukraine and taken to Hungary, Kloop reported. Source link : https://kloop.kg/blog/2022/03/01/evakuatsiya-iz-ukrainy-bolee-sta-kyrgyzstantsev-doehali-do-vengrii/
Headline: Kyrgyzstan evacuates its nationals from Ukraine

Description: In Mariupol, Donetsk oblast, Ukraine, Greece sets a humanitarian convoy of 21 vehicles evacuating 82 Greek nationals from Ukraine. The convoy is set off to the southeastern Zaporizhzhya region where it would cross into Moldova on the way to Greece, Ekathemerini reported. Source link : https://www.ekathimerini.com/news/1178804/humanitarian-corridor-for-greek-expatriates-in-mariupol/
Headline: Greece evacuates its nationals from Ukraine

Description: In Moscow, Russia, Russian presidential spokesperson Dmitry Peskov stated that Russia would not comply with the recent binding ruling of the International Court of Justice, which obliged the country to 'immediately suspend' its military invasion of Ukraine, the Moscow Times reported. Source link : https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2022/03/17/kremlin-rejects-top-un-courts-order-to-halt-ukraine-invasion-a76975
Headline: Russian presidential spokesperson declines to comply with the International Court of Justice order

Description: In Paris, Île-de-France, France, the French Minister of Foreign Affairs Jean Yves Le Drian accused Russia of only pretending to seek a peace deal with Ukraine, Tele Trade reported. Source link : https://www.teletrade.eu/analytics/news/3738074
Headline: France accuses Russia of the pretension of a peace deal with Ukraine

Description: In Tokyo, Japan, Japanese government announced sanctions against Russia, banning the issuance of Russian bonds in Japan, freezing assets of certain Russian nationals, prohibiting their entry to Japan, and restricting trade with Ukraine’s Donetsk and Luhansk separatist republics, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/politics/23/02/2022/621589199a7947f23ea36fe4?from=newsfeed
Headline: Japanese government announces sanctions against Russia

Description: In Ottawa city, Ontario Province, Canada, Canadian government announced sanctions on Russia, banning Canadian nationals from purchasing Russian sovereign debt, prohibiting trade with Ukraine’s Donetsk and Luhansk separatist regions. They introduced individuals sanctions on Russian Members of Parliament, Global News reported. Source link : https://globalnews.ca/news/8637670/canada-sanctions-ukraine-russia/
Headline: Canadian government announces sanctions against Russia

Description: In Washington D.C., District of Columbia, United States (US), the US Secretary of State Antony Blinken cancelled a bilateral meeting with Russia's Minister of Foreign Affairs Sergeĭ Viktorovich Lavrov, scheduled for 24 February 2022, following Russia’s recognition of Ukraine’s (DPR) and (LPR) separatists, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/politics/23/02/2022/62155ba89a7947e9475f7e6c?from=from_main_1
Headline: US Secretary of State cancels a bilateral meeting with Russian Minister of Foreign Affairs Minister

Description: In Canberra, Australian Capital Territory, Australia, government imposed sanctions against eight members of Russia’s Security Council, five state banks, and banned Australian nationals from investing in Russia’s VEB corporation and trading with Ukraine’s Donetsk People's Republic (DPR) and Luhansk People's Republic (LPR) separatist republics, the Guardian reported. Source link : https://www.theguardian.com/australia-news/2022/feb/23/australia-imposes-sanctions-on-russia-in-response-to-ukraine-crisis
Headline: Australia government announces sanction against Russia

Description: In Moscow city, Moskova, Russian Federation, Federal Air Transport Agency (Rosaviatsia) restricted operations of 11 Russian airports located in southern regions bordering Ukraine, and an airport in annexed Crimea until 2 March 2022, Interfax reported. Source link : https://www.interfax.ru/russia/824042
Headline: Russian Federal Air Transport Agency restrict operations of Russian airports in southern regions

Description: At Belarus embassy, Kyiv, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, Ambassador of Belarus to Ukraine Igor Sokol stated that Belarus evacuated 11 Belarusian diplomats and their family members. The diplomats complained of excessive checking by Ukrainian border guards at the borders. He also said that these diplomats were evacuated to Moldova as they were not guaranteed security in the direction of Belarus, TCH reported. Source link : https://tsn.ua/ru/ukrayina/vse-belorusskie-diplomaty-uehali-iz-ukrainy-2014213.html
Headline: Belarus evacuates its diplomats to Moldova from Ukraine

Description: At Verkhovna Rada, Kyiv, Ukraine, parliament supported a motion to request the country’s National Security and Defence Council to introduce 10-year sanctions against 351 Russian Members of Parliament (MPs), who had supported Russia’s recognition of Donetsk and Luhansk region, Hromadske reported. Source link : https://hromadske.ua/posts/verhovna-rada-pidtrimala-sankciyi-proti-deputativ-derzhdumi-rf-yaki-pidtrimali-viznannya-ldnr
Headline: Ukrainian parliament supports a motion to sanction Russian MPs

Description: At White House, District of Columbia, United States (US), the US government ruled out holding a bilateral summit with Russian government unless Russia contributed in de-escalating the political and military crisis around Ukraine, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/politics/23/02/2022/62156cc39a7947eb75c515b0?from=newsfeed
Headline: US White House rules out holding a bilateral summit with Russia

Description: At the International Criminal Court (ICC), The Hague, Netherlands, the ICC announced that Ukraine filed a lawsuit against Russia. ICC added that they might launch investigations into possible war crimes in Ukraine against Russia, Reuters reported. Source link : https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/icc-says-may-investigate-possible-war-crimes-after-russian-invasion-ukraine-2022-02-25/?taid=6218c011af8d2b0001567a75&utm_campaign=trueAnthem:+Trending+Content&utm_medium=trueAnthem&utm_source=twitter
Headline: Ukraine files a lawsuit in the ICC against Russia

Description: In Washington, District of Columbia, United States, the US Department of State introduced sanctions against 11 Russian nationals, mostly high-ranking public officials from the Ministry of Defence, for their alleged implication in Russian invasion of Ukraine, the US Department of State reported. Source link : https://www.state.gov/u-s-announces-sanctions-on-key-members-of-russias-defense-enterprise/
Headline: US sanctions 11 Russian officials over Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: in Canberra, Australian Capital Territory, Australia, the government imposed a ban on the exports of alumina and aluminum ores, including bauxite, to Russia. The government stated that Russia relies on Australia for nearly 20 percent of its alumina needs, Reuters reported. Source link : https://www.reuters.com/world/australia-bans-alumina-exports-russia-sources-coal-ukraine-2022-03-19/
Headline: Australia bans alumina exports to Russia

Description: In Washington, D.C., District of Columbia, United States, World Bank announced suspension of all programmes in Russia and Belarus effective immediately due to Russian invasion in Ukraine. The invasion is assisted and supported by Belarus, World Bank reported. Source link : https://www.worldbank.org/en/news/statement/2022/03/02/world-bank-group-statement-on-russia-and-belarus
Headline: World Bank suspends all programmes in Russia and Belarus

Description: In The Hague, Netherlands, the Prosecutor Office of the International Criminal Court (ICC) announced plans to request an ICC approval to launch investigations against Russia into possible war crimes committed in Ukraine, Reuters reported. Source link : https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/icc-prosecutor-proceed-with-investigation-into-alleged-war-crimes-ukraine-2022-02-28/
Headline: ICC prosecutor investigates war crimes in Ukraine

Description: In Hague, Netherlands, around 39 signatories of the Rome Statute, including Canada and France, petitioned the International Criminal Court (ICC) to launch an investigation into possible Russian war crimes committed in Ukraine. The ICC has agreed to launch an investigation, Aljazeera reported. Source link : https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/3/2/icc-to-begin-investigation-into-possible-war-crimes-in-ukraine
Headline: Countries petition court to launch investigation on Russian war crimes in Ukraine

Description: In Kyiv city, Kyiv, Ukraine, parliament tabled a decree proposing to close Ukraine’s state border with Russia and Belarus to introduce martial law in Donetsk and Luhansk regions, and to sever transport connections with Russia, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/politics/23/02/2022/6215ef1c9a79470791fa26f3?from=newsfeed
Headline: Ukraine’s parliament tables decree proposing to close Ukraine's border with Russia and Belarus

Description: In Tallinn, Estonia, Minister of Foreign Affairs Eva-Maria Liimets announced the suspension of the issuance of C and D tourist category visas to Russian nationals for the whole duration of Russia’s military invasion in Ukraine, CNN reported. Source link : https://edition.cnn.com/europe/live-news/ukraine-russia-putin-news-03-10-22/h_4411ec2b5580fdf48654fe93648a42c6
Headline: Estonia suspends issuance of tourist visas to Russian nationals

Description: In Warsaw, Poland, President Andrzej Duda urged for tougher sanctions against Russia in retaliation of Russia’s attack on a maternity hospital in Ukraine, described by Duda as an “act of barbarity and genocide.”, CNN reported. Source link : https://edition.cnn.com/europe/live-news/ukraine-russia-putin-news-03-10-22/h_a13456e950b63a00912714edbf05f721
Headline: Poland’s President urges for tougher sanctions against Russia

Description: In Brussels, Belgium, the European Union urged Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele and his government to defend Ukraine against Russia's invasion. He urged El Salvador to join other nations to support Russia's isolation due to its actions in Ukraine, La Prensa Grafica reported. Source link : https://www.laprensagrafica.com/elsalvador/Union-Europea-senala-el-silencio-de-El-Salvador-sobre-invasion-rusa-a-Ucrania-20220228-0056.html
Headline: EU urges El Salvador for a stance against Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In Donetsk city, Donetsk, Ukraine, the Donetsk People's Republic (DPR) and Luhansk People's Republic (LPR) separatist leaders formally requested Russia’s assistance in what they described as repelling Ukraine’s military attack against the civilian population, RIA reported. Source link : https://ria.ru/20220223/agressiya-1774600394.html?fbclid=IwAR3p1G-gSwfCWgUerFt-ZeXjnA_dtjMhqlGb-gW8D6coDFr7EElW2SssqqI
Headline: Ukraine’s DPR and LPR separatist leaders formally request Russia’s assistance

Description: In Buenos Aires, Argentina, Foreign Affairs Minister Santiago Cafiero announced the situation of Argentinian nationals residing in Ukraine. He confirmed that 58 Argentinian nationals managed to leave the country amid Russian invasion of Ukraine, Ambito reported. Source link : https://www.ambito.com/politica/conflicto-rusia-ucrania/mas-argentinos-lograron-salir-ucrania-n5383835
Headline: Argentine Foreign Minister announces the situation of its nationals in Ukraine

Description: At White House, Washington D.C., District of Columbia, United States (US), Joe Biden Presidential Administration informed Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyi on Russia’s plans to launch a full-scale military invasion in Ukraine within the next 48 hours, Newsweek reported. Source link : https://www.newsweek.com/exclusive-us-warns-ukraine-full-scale-russian-invasion-within-48-hours-1681798
Headline: US presidential administration informed Ukraine’s President on Russia’s plans to invasion

Description: In Mariupol, Donetsk region, Ukraine, Greek Consulate General was moved to a different location in Mariupol due to security reasons after the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Kathimerini reported. Source link : https://www.ekathimerini.com/news/1178729/new-contact-numbers-for-mariupol-consulate-general/
Headline: Greek government relocates its embassy in Ukraine

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, the Italian embassy in Ukraine had been moved from Kyiv to Lviv due to the deteriorating security situation in the country. Ambassador Pier Francesco Zazo also rescue around 20 children including six newborn babies, ANSA reported. Source link : https://www.ansa.it/english/news/general_news/2022/03/01/ukraine-italy-embassy-moved-to-lviv-envoy-rescues-20-kids_6f1f11a4-c5c9-4488-92f7-495a3dfdf186.html
Headline: Italy relocates its embassy in Ukraine

Description: In The Hague, Netherlands, the International Criminal Court (ICC) scheduled the first public hearing for 7-8 March 2022 to review Ukraine’s allegation of genocide lodged against Russia, Barrons reported. Source link : https://www.barrons.com/news/icj-to-hold-ukraine-war-hearings-on-march-7-8-court-01646161208
Headline: ICC schedules hearing to review allegation of genocide against Russia

Description: In London, England, United Kingdom, Prime Minister Boris Johnson agreed to accept over 200,000 Ukrainian refugees whose family members had been already residing in the UK amid Russia's invasion of Ukraine, Sky News reported. Source link : https://news.sky.com/story/ukraine-invasion-boris-johnson-says-uk-stands-ready-to-take-refugees-fleeing-fighting-in-considerable-numbers-12554657
Headline: UK Prime Minister agrees to accept Ukrainian refugees

Description: In Melbourne, Victoria, Australia, government denounced the deployment of soldiers by Russia after declaring Ukraine's Donetsk and Luhansk regions as independent states and announced plans to impose sanctions on Russia by coordinating with other countries. The government also urged its nationals to leave Ukraine immediately, Ministry of Foreign Affairs reported. Source link : https://www.foreignminister.gov.au/minister/marise-payne/media-release/australia-condemns-russias-unlawful-moves-eastern-ukraine
Headline: Australian government denounces Russia's declaration on Ukraine

Description: In Kherson, Ukraine, the Russian forces have kidnapped a local councilor of Ukraine. He was the leader of the European Solidarity party, BBC Monitoring reported. Source link : https://monitoring.bbc.co.uk/product/c203cccv
Headline: Russian forces kidnap Ukraine councilor

Description: In Slavutych, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, thousands of residents protested and released a mayor. The Russian forces had entered the city seized a hospital and took the mayor hostage. The protestors pushed the Russian Forces back to the outskirts of town, New York Post reported. Source link : https://nypost.com/2022/03/26/ukraine-mayor-taken-hostage-released-after-protesters-push-troops-back/
Headline: Civilians protest and release a mayor taken hostage by Russian forces in Ukraine's Slavutych

Description: In Ottawa, Ontario, Canada, Minister of Foreign Affairs Mélanie Joly said that the Canadian government would carry on working with international partners to help and safeguard Ukraine through aid and diplomacy. She further said it would be based on its limited capabilities, National Post reported. Source link : https://nationalpost.com/news/politics/jolys-what-were-good-at-is-convening-comments-sell-canadian-military-short-generals-say
Headline: Canadian Minister of Foreign Affairs ensures foreign aid to help Ukraine

Description: In Buenos Aires, Argentina, during a virtual meeting with the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights Michelle Bachelet, President Alberto Fernandez reiterated his firm rejection of the use of Russian armed force in Ukraine and called for cessation of hostilities. They exchanged views on the human rights situation at the global level and also on the escalation of war in Ukraine. He also stressed that the path should be followed with dialogue and negotiation, Infobae reported. Source link : https://www.infobae.com/politica/2022/02/27/alberto-fernandez-reitero-su-rechazo-al-uso-de-la-fuerza-armada-rusa-en-ucrania/
Headline: Argentine President discusses Russian invasion of Ukraine with UN High Commissioner for Human Rights

Description: In Buenos Aires, Argentina, the government rejected the resolution issued by the Organisation of American States (OAS) that condemned the Russian invasion of Ukraine. The government called the resolution as "illegal, unjustified and unprovoked". The resolution was driven by the United States which received support of 21 countries, La Politica Online reported. Source link : https://www.lapoliticaonline.com/internacionales/el-gobierno-no-acompano-un-repudio-a-putin-impulsado-por-estados-unidos-en-la-oea/
Headline: Argentine government rejects the resolution of OAS that condemned Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In Buenos Aires, Argentina, Foreign Minister Santiago Cafiero condemned Russia's invasion of Ukraine at the United Nations Human Rights Council. He urged both countries to resume dialogue to resolve the conflict. He reiterated his call for dialogue to end the escalation of war in Ukriane. He emphasised that diplomacy should be used to discuss conflicts and prevent any escalation further, La Nacion reported. Source link : https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/santiago-cafiero-la-argentina-condena-la-invasion-a-ucrania-nid28022022/
Headline: Argentine Foreign Minister condemns Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In Buenos Aires, Argentina, President Alberto Fernández decided to condemn Russia's invasion of Ukraine at a meeting of the United Nations' General Assembly scheduled for 28 February 2022. Earlier, Foriegn Minister condemned Russian's invasion of Ukraine at the Human Rights Commission, Infobae reported. Source link : https://www.infobae.com/politica/2022/02/28/alberto-fernandez-profundiza-sus-diferencias-con-cfk-y-condenara-la-invasion-de-rusia-a-ucrania-en-la-onu/
Headline: Argentine President condemns Russia's invasion of Ukraine

Description: In Bucharest, Romania, President Klaus Werner Iohannis visits mobile camp for refugees in Siret, Suceava County. The camps were erected after the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Presidency Romania reported. Source link :
Headline: Romania's President reports on the refugee camps

Description: In Vienna, Austria, Minister of Energy Leonore Gewessler announced that Russian gas deliveries to Austria were continuing despite the Russian invasion of Ukraine. The announcement was made after a meeting with experts from E-Control, the Energy Agency and the Ministry, Österreichischer Rundfunk reported. Source link : https://orf.at/#/stories/3248938/
Headline: Austrian Minister of Energy announces Russian gas deliveries to Austria

Description: In Prague, Czech Republic, the Czech Ministry of Foreign Affairs decided that the Czech embassy in Kyiv would remain open, despite recent tensions over the Russian military build-up on the Ukraine-Russia border, Radio Prague reported. Source link : https://english.radio.cz/russian-ambassador-summoned-foreign-ministry-over-ukraine-situation-8742770
Headline: Czech government announces that its embassy in Ukraine remains open

Description: In Warsaw, Polish President Andrzej Duda met with Romanian Prime Minister Nicolae Ionel Ciuca to discuss aid to Ukraine, as well as the Black Sea and Adriatic Sea security and the possibility of fast-tracking Ukraine’s European Union accession application, The First News reported. Source link : https://www.thefirstnews.com/article/president-romanian-pm-discuss-ukraine-28415
Headline: Polish President meets Romanian Prime Minister to discuss aid to Ukraine

Description:
In Warsaw, Mazowieckie, Poland, the Border Service stated that nearly 1 million Ukrainian refugees had entered the country since the beginning of the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Polsat News reported.
 Source link : https://www.polsatnews.pl/wiadomosc/2022-03-06/straz-graniczna-ponad-964-tys-uchodzcow-z-ukrainy-przekroczyly-granice-polski/?ref=1491573
Headline: Polish Border Service briefs on Ukrainian refugees in the country

Description: In Bratislava, Slovakia, the Slovak Police recorded that so far, some 220,000 Ukrainian refugees had entered Slovakia since the launch of Russian invasion of Ukraine, Spectator reported. Source link : https://spectator.sme.sk/c/22862884/news-digest-pm-misses-train-to-kyiv-activists-bring-tractor-to-move-out-russian-spies.html
Headline: Slovak Police says 220,000 Ukrainian refugees had entered the country

Description: In Jerusalem, Israel, Foreign Minister Yair Lapid the Russian military invasion of Ukraine. The Minister stated that the attack was a violation of the international order. Moreover, he added that Israeli representatives were present in Moldova, Romania, Hungary, Slovakia, and Poland, to facilitate the movement of Israeli citizens from Ukraine, Times of Israel reported. Source link :
Headline: Israel condemns Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In Washington, D.C., District of Columbia, United States, the government released a note criticising Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro's stance during his visit to Russia when he declared solidarity with that country amid Russia-Ukraine tensions, Info Money reported. Source link : https://www.infomoney.com.br/politica/eua-criticam-solidariedade-de-bolsonaro-a-russia-e-acusa-pais-de-enfraquecer-esforcos-diplomaticos/
Headline: US government criticises Brazilian president's support to Russia

Description: In Washington D.C., the United States, the Senate averted a government shutdown and passed a bill by 68-31 votes. The bill includes USD1.5 trillion for government funding and USD13.6 billion in humanitarian aid to Ukraine, the Hill reported. Source link : https://thehill.com/homenews/senate/597797-senate-passes-sweeping-bill-to-fund-government-provide-ukraine-aid
Headline: US Senate averts a government shutdown and passed a bill

Description: In Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, the Chairperson of the African Union (AU) Macky Sall urged the Russian Federation and Ukraine to establish an immediate cease-fire and to open political negotiations, according to the African Union website. Source link :
Headline: AU expresses concern over the Ukrainian conflict

Description: In Voronezh, Russian Federation, Governor Aleksandr Gusev declared a state of emergency due to the increased flow of refugees from Ukraine’s Donetsk and Luhansk, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/politics/20/02/2022/6212226e9a79473806155723?from=newsfeed
Headline: Governor declares a state of emergency in Russia's Voronezh

Description: In Moscow, Russian Federation, Russia’s parliament stated that Russia’s immigration offices had been currently reviewing citizenship applications of over 170,000 Ukrainian nationals, living in Ukraine’s Donetsk People's Republic (DPR) and Luhansk People's Republic (LPR) breakaway territories. Over 770,000 Donbas residents have already obtained Russian citizenship, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/rbcfreenews/6211f1109a79472d47ecaa95
Headline: Russia’s immigration offices review citizenship applications from Donetsk and Luhansk regions

Description: In Moscow, Russia’s Emergency Situations Minister Alexander Chupriyan reported that over 40,000 residents of Ukraine’s Donetsk People's Republic (DPR) and Luhansk People's Republic (LPR) breakaway territories had been evacuated to Russia’s Rostov region amid the military escalation in Ukraine’s Donbas region, TASS reported. Source link : https://tass.com/society/1406599
Headline: Russia evacuates its civilians from Donetsk and Luhansk regions

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, the presidential office estimated that Ukraine had been sustaining financial losses amounting to USD2-3 billion per month due to the ongoing security threats stemming from Russia’s military build-up near Ukrainian borders, Hromadske reported. Source link : https://hromadske.ua/posts/v-ofisi-prezidenta-rozpovili-skilki-shomisyacya-vtrachaye-ukrayina-cherez-rosijsku-agresiyu
Headline: Presidential office reports about Ukraine's financial losses due to security threats

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, President Volodymyr Zelenskyi announced a call of reservists of the armed forces and the territorial defence battalions, but ruled out a general mobilisation, in the context of Russia's decision to introduce troops into the occupied Donbas region, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ua/rus/news/zelenskiy-obyavil-prizyv-rezervistov-vsu-1645561573.html
Headline: President calls for reservists of the armed forces in Ukraine's Kyiv

Description: In Moscow, Russia, President Vladimir Putin claimed that Russia had identified the criminals involved in the 2014 clashes in Ukraine's city Odesa, which resulted in the deaths of 48 civilians, and that Russia would seek to prosecute these criminals, Interfax reported. Source link : https://www.interfax.ru/russia/823524
Headline: Russia claims to have identified criminals responsible for clashes in Ukraine in 2014

Description: In Moscow, Russia, Kazakhstan's President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev met with President Vladimir Putin to discuss the ongoing situation in Ukraine. Putin reaffirmed Russia’s intention of strengthening multi-faceted cooperation with Kazakhstan, Kazakh telegraph agency reported. Source link : https://kaztag.kz/en/news/putin-in-conversation-with-tokayev-confirmed-commitment-to-alliance-with-kazakhstan
Headline: Kazakhstan's President discusses the ongoing situation in Ukraine with Russian president

Description:
In Kyiv, Ukraine, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs requested President Volodymyr Zelenskyi to sever diplomatic relations with Russia. Russia’s Ministry of Foreign 
affairs stated that Russia supported the continuation of bilateral diplomatic ties with Ukraine's authorities, Lenta reported. Source link : https://lenta.ru/news/2022/02/22/mid_otkaz/
Headline: Ukraine's Ministry of Foreign Affairs urges President Volodymyr Zelenskyi to sever diplomatic relations with Russia

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, President Volodymyr Zelenskyi requested Germany, France and Russia to hold an emergency summit of the Normandy Four format on the Donbas conflict settlement, Interfax reported. Source link : https://www.interfax.ru/world/823570
Headline: Ukraine urges Germany, France and Russia to hold an emergency summit for Donbas conflict settlement

Description: In Minsk, Belarus, domestic bank BelVEB fell under the United States sanctions against Russia over the recognition of Ukraine's Luhansk and Donetsk regions independence, as BelVEB was listed as linked to Russia’s VEB development cooperation, Reform reported. Source link : https://reform.by/298455-bank-belvjeb-popal-pod-sankcii-ssha
Headline: US sanctions Belarusian BelVEB bank

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, leaders of the Donetsk People's Republic (DPR) and Luhansk People's Republic (LPR) separatist republics separately stated intentions to expand their territorial control within the borders of Ukraine’s administrative Donetsk and Luhansk regions, Novyny reported. Source link : https://novyny.live/ru/politics/pushilin-pozarilsia-na-podkontrolnye-ukraine-territorii-v-donetskoi-oblasti-i-khochet-novykh-granits-40560.html
Headline: LPR and DPR leaders state their intentions to expand borders within Ukraine’s administrative regions

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs recalled the country’s Chargé d'affaires Vasily Pokotilo in Russia to Kyiv to discuss the political consequences of Russia’s diplomatic recognition of Ukraine’s separatist Donetsk People's Republic (DPR) and Luhansk People's Republic (LPR) claimed territories, RIA Novosti reported. Source link : https://ria.ru/20220222/ukraina-1774346095.html
Headline: Ukraine's Ministry of Foreign Affairs recalls the country's Chargé d'affaires to Russia

Description: In Tbilisi, Georgia, Georgia’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs recommended that embassy staff in Kyiv evacuate along with their families from Ukraine, Agenda reported. Source link : https://agenda.ge/en/news/2022/393
Headline: Georgian government advises its embassy staff to leave Ukraine amid the ongoing crisis

Description: In Moscow, Russia, Ministry of Foreign Affairs commenced an evacuation of all staff members, working in Russia’s embassy in Ukraine’s capital of Kyiv and its consulates in the cities of Kharkiv, Odesa and Lviv, to their residence country, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/politics/23/02/2022/62161a5e9a79471495d8ddb3?from=newsfeed
Headline: Russia evacuates staff members from its embassy in Ukraine

Description:
In Moscow, Russia, the presidential spokesperson Dmitry Peskov unveiled Russian President Vladimir 
Putin’s readiness to enter into negotiations with Ukrainian authorities conditioned on the country’s demilitarisation and commitment not to join the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO), Politico reported. Source link : https://www.politico.eu/article/vladimir-putin-moscow-negotiate-kyiv-ukraine-russia-neutrality/
Headline: Russian spokesperson announces readiness for negotiations with Ukrainian authorities

Description: In Kyiv, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, the government significantly expanded the list of imported goods considered as critically important and established a coordinate centre tasked with ensuring the delivery of food stuff, medicine, drinking water and fuel to war-affected settlements across the country, Unian reported. Source link : https://www.unian.net/economics/finance/kabmin-prinyal-vazhnye-resheniya-naschet-importa-i-obespecheniya-pishchey-lekarstvami-i-vodoy-novosti-ukraina-11722765.html
Headline: Ukraine expands list of critical import goods

Description: In Moscow, Russia, communication sector regulator Federal Service for Supervision of Communications, Information Technology and Mass Media (Rokomnadzor) restricted access to the websites of the British public media broadcaster BBC, Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty and Russia’s Latvia-based Meduza, the Guardian reported. Source link : https://www.theguardian.com/media/2022/mar/04/bbc-website-blocked-in-russia-as-shortwave-radio-brought-back-to-cover-ukraine-war
Headline: Russia restricts access to BBC, US Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty and Meduza

Description:
In London, England, United Kingdom (UK), the government advised all citizens whose presence was not essential in Russia to leave the country immediately amid the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Daily Mail reported.
 Source link : https://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-10580573/Government-tells-British-nationals-Russia-presence-not-essential.html
Headline: UK government advises citizens in Russia to leave the country

Description:
In Kyiv, Ukraine, the government confirmed the successful evacuation of civilians from the Enerhodar in Donetsk via a Russia-backed humanitarian corridor, News Sky reported.
 Source link : https://news.sky.com/story/ukraine-russia-war-latest-news-putin-zelenskyy-ceasefire-nato-visa-live-updates-12541713?postid=3507329#liveblog-body
Headline: Ukraine confirms evacuation of civilians from Donetsk

Description:
In Kyiv, Ukraine, Minister of Foreign Dmytro Kuleba expressed low expectations for the bilateral meeting with the Russian Minister of Foreign Sergei Lavrov scheduled for 10 March 2022, CNN reported.
 Source link : https://edition.cnn.com/europe/live-news/ukraine-russia-putin-news-03-09-22/h_978c3bece371ec12723285d758f79115
Headline: Ukrainian Minister of Foreign Affairs expresses low expectations for the meeting with his Russian counterpart

Description:
In Minsk, Belarus, the Minister of Foreign Affairs Uladzimir Makey said that Russia and Ukraine should get rid of external advisors during the peace talks, according to the Belarus Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
 Source link :
Headline: Belarusian government states that Russia and Ukraine should forego external advisors

Description:
In Kyiv, Ukraine, the government relocated the staff of the Kazakh embassy to Lviv. The decision was taken due to the worsening situation in the capital city amid the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Tengri News reported.
 Source link :
Headline: Government relocates Kazakh Embassy staff in Ukraine’s Kyiv

Description:
In Kyiv, Ukraine, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy urged countries to impose a complete trade embargo on Russia and its complete disconnection from the international SWIFT payment system. He said the current sanctions are insufficient, according to the President of Ukraine.
 Source link :
Headline: Ukrainian President calls for a complete trade embargo on Russia

Description: In Buenos Aires, Argentina, the government announced that the Extended Fund Facility with the International Monetary Fund (IMF) would include a special clause. This clause will allow reviewing the goals set in the agreement should the war in Ukraine modify economic variables, Ambito reported. Source link : https://www.ambito.com/economia/fmi/por-la-guerra-ucrania-gobierno-y-negocian-clausula-especial-n5382062
Headline: IMF includes a special clause in Extended Fund Facility with Argentina

Description: In Quito, Pichincha province, Ecuador, Corporation of Exporters Unions of Ecuador (CORDEX) expressed concern about retaliatory sanctions against Russia due to the invasion of Ukraine, considering the export of several non-oil products to that region, Ecuavisa reported. Source link : https://www.ecuavisa.com/noticias/economia/la-guerra-en-ucrania-ya-afecta-las-exportaciones-de-ecuador-a-rusia-KH1393905
Headline: Ecuadorian exporter unions express their concerns over sanctions against Russia

Description: In New Delhi, National Capital Territory of Delhi.
