India, several union ministers, including Minister of Petroleum Hardeep Singh Puri, Minister of Civil Aviation Jyotiraditya Scindia and Minister of Roads and Transport Gen V K Singh would be travelling to Romania, Moldova, and Poland respectively, to speed-up evacuation for Indian citizens stranded in Ukraine amid Russian invasion, Hindustan Times reported. Source link : https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/russia-ukraine-indian-ministers-to-leave-for-countries-bordering-ukraine-tonight-101646031813107.html
Headline: Indian minister leave for Europe to evacuate Indian nationals from Ukraine

Description: In New Delhi, National Capital Territory of Delhi, India, Foreign Secretary Harsh Vardhan Shringla claimed that all Indian nationals had left the capital city Kyiv, Ukraine. India is planning to evacuate its nationals from other cities in the conflict zone after Russian invasion of the country, the Times of India reported. Source link : https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/all-indians-have-left-ukraine-capital-kyiv-foreign-secretary/articleshow/89930217.cms
Headline: Indian government briefs on citizen evacuation from Ukraine

Description: In Vienna, Austria, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs advised Austrian nationals residing in Ukraine to immediately leave the country, with the exception of those residing in Lviv, Zakarpattia, Ivano-Frankivsk and Chernivtsi regions, RIA Novosti reported. Source link : https://ria.ru/20220219/avstriya-1773796453.html
Headline: Austrian government advises its nationals to leave Ukraine amid the ongoing crisis

Description: In London, England, United Kingdom, Labour Party leader Sir Keir Starmer warned the party ministers that if any of them criticised or blamed North Atlantic Treaty Organisation (NATO) for the Russian military invasion of Ukraine they would be expelled from the party, as Labour's commitment would be to democracy and the rule of law, Independent reported. Source link : https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/politics/starmer-labour-mps-nato-russia-b2025240.html
Headline: UK's Labour party warns members against criticising NATO

Description: In Yerevan city, Yerevan Province, Armenia, the Armenian and Azerbaijani Members of Parliament (MPs) met with mediators from the European Union (EU), Ukraine, and Georgia to discuss issues surrounding the breakaway region of Nagorno-Karabakh. Officials discussed return of prisoners of war, territorial integrity, and the protection of cultural heritage in the region, JAM News reported. Source link : https://jam-news.net/azerbaijani-mps-in-yerevan-euronest-meeting-amid-protests/
Headline: Armenian and Azerbaijani MPs meet with mediators to discuss issues in Nagorno-Karabakh region

Description: In Kyiv city, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, the Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky recalled the Ambassador to Georgia over the ruling Georgian Dream – Democratic Georgia party’s (Kartuli ocneba – Demok’rat’iuli Sakartvelo) refusal to participate in sanctions against Russia and to allow volunteers to fly to Ukraine to participate in the defence of the country, JAM News reported. Source link : https://jam-news.net/ukraine-recalls-ambassador-to-georgia-amid-immoral-position-of-georgian-authorities/
Headline: Ukrainian President recalls the Ambassador to Georgia

Description: In Moscow, Russian Federation, Ukraine Ambassador to Russia Minister of State for Immigration and Egyptians Abroad Affairs Nabila Makram Abdel-Shaheed held a meeting via the 'Zoom' application with representatives of Egyptian students. The meeting was to check students on their conditions in light of the developments of the Russian-Ukrainian crisis. The political leadership’s directives was to follow up on our children abroad and reassure for their safety, Youm7 reported. Source link : https://www.youm7.com/story/2022/3/22/الهجرة-طرح-استمارة-تسجيل-موحدة-للطلاب-المصريين-الدارسين-فى-روسيا/5700523
Headline: Egyptian Minister of State for Immigration meets Egyptian students studying in Russia

Description: In Prague, Hlavní město Praha, Czech Republic, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs advised Czech nationals to leave Ukraine without delay in light of tensions over the Russian military build-up on the Ukraine-Russia border, English Radio reported. Source link : https://english.radio.cz/czech-foreign-ministry-issues-fresh-appeal-czechs-leave-ukraine-8742581
Headline: Czech Ministry of Foreign Affairs issues advisory urging it's nationals to leave Ukraine

Description: In Bratislava, Slovak, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs advised Slovak nationals to leave the Ukrainian Luhansk, Donetsk, Zaporizhzhia and Kherson regions without delay in light of recent tensions over the Russian military build-up on the Ukraine-Russia border, Spectator reported. Source link : https://spectator.sme.sk/c/22846262/news-digest-slovakia-remembers-journalists-murder.html
Headline: Slovak Ministry of Foreign Affairs issues advisory urging Slovak nationals  leave the Ukrainian region

Description: In Prague, Hlavní město Praha region, Czech Republic, the Prime Minister Petr Fiala discuss a request to the Chamber of Deputies to extend a state of emergency.  This was imposed to tackle refugees from Ukraine, Radio Czech Republic reported. Source link : https://english.radio.cz/cabinet-discuss-move-extend-state-emergency-8745579
Headline: Czech Republic Prime Minister plans to extend the state of emergency

Description: In Istanbul, Turkey, a senior Turkish official announced hosting peace talks between Ukraine and Russia that may take place. The Kremlin announced that the peace talks between Russia and Ukraine may start in Turkey on  29 March 2022, pointing out that it is important that the talks be held face to face, despite the little progress in the negotiations so far, Alaraby reported. Source link : https://www.alaraby.co.uk/politics/%D8%A5%D8%B3%D8%B7%D9%86%D8%A8%D9%88%D9%84-%D8%AA%D8%B3%D8%AA%D8%B6%D9%8A%D9%81-%D9%85%D8%AD%D8%A7%D8%AF%D8%AB%D8%A7%D8%AA-%D8%B3%D9%84%D8%A7%D9%85-%D8%A8%D9%8A%D9%86-%D8%A3%D9%88%D9%83%D8%B1%D8%A7%D9%86%D9%8A%D8%A7-%D9%88%D8%B1%D9%88%D8%B3%D9%8A%D8%A7-%D9%82%D8%AF-%D8%AA%D8%B9%D9%82%D8%AF-%D8%A7%D9%84%D9%8A%D9%88%D9%85-%D8%A3%D9%88-%D8%BA%D8%AF%D8%A7
Headline: Turkey announces hosting peace talks between Ukraine and Russia

Description: In Moscow, Russian Federation, Presidential spokesperson Dmitry Peskov stated that Russia did not see the point in a proposed meeting between President Vladimir Vladimirovich Putin and his Ukrainian counterpart Volodymyr Zelenskyi, repeatedly suggested by Ukraine, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/politics/17/02/2022/620e369b9a79478269c92e12?from=newsfeed
Headline: Russia's Presidential spokesperson did not see the point of a meeting between President and his Ukrainian counterpart

Description: In Moscow, Russian Federation, ruling party United Russia (Yedinaya Rossiya) proposed to nationalise foreign-owned manufacturing property assets. The assets of companies that had or would decide to suspend their operations in Russia due to Russian invasion in Ukraine, DP reported. Source link : https://www.dp.ru/a/2022/03/07/Edinaja_Rossija_predlozhil
Headline: Russia's ruling party proposes to nationalise foreign-owned assets of companies in the country

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, the Cabinet of Ministers announced it would provide special working conditions for enterprises. The plans was to evacuate their businesses from war-affected territories into western regions, Epravda reported. Source link : https://www.epravda.com.ua/rus/news/2022/03/8/683523/
Headline: Ukraine government announces evacuation of enterprises into western regions

Description: In Moscow, Russian Federation, Russian government and Ukraine government said the negotiations were becoming more realistic and the proposals that were being discussed are close to an agreement. President Vladimir Putin also said Russia would achieve its goals in Ukraine and would not submit to what he called a Western attempt to achieve global dominance and dismember Russia, Dhaka Tribune reported. Source link : https://www.dhakatribune.com/europe/2022/03/16/both-sides-in-russia-ukraine-conflict-talk-of-compromise-putin-says-operation-going-to-plan
Headline: Russia and Ukraine talk about compromising

Description: In Moscow, Russian Federation, National Defence Control Centre Mikhail Mizintsev claimed that Ukraine rejected humanitarian corridors. Mizintsev said that the authorities of Ukraine have not approved any of the four humanitarian corridors proposed by the Russian side, BBC Monitoring reported. Source link : https://monitoring.bbc.co.uk/product/c203c829
Headline: Russia claims Ukraine rejected humanitarian corridors

Description: In Athens, Attica region, Greece, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs issued a travel advisory to its citizens, advising them against travelling to Ukraine, and urged its citizens in Ukraine in need of assistance to contact the Greek Embassy in Kyiv and consular representations in Mariupol and Odessa, Ekathimerini reported. Source link : https://www.ekathimerini.com/news/1178061/foreign-ministry-advises-greeks-to-avoid-travelling-to-ukraine/
Headline: Greece's Ministry of Foreign Affairs issue a travel advisory to citizens in Ukraine

Description: In Athens, Attica region, Greece, Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis pledged rebuilding the bombed maternity hospitals in Ukraine's Mariupol. The rebuilding would execute once the hostilities were over, Ekathimerini reported. Source link : https://www.ekathimerini.com/news/1180101/greece-offers-to-rebuild-bombed-maternity-hospital-in-ukraine/
Headline: Greek PM announces rebuilding the bombed hospital in Ukraine

Description: In Athens, Attica region, Greece, Prime Minister Konstantinos Mitsotakis condemned the killing of two ethnic Greeks in Ukraine's Sartana, Mariupol, and four others in Buhas, Donetsk region, during the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Ekathimerini reported. Source link : https://www.ekathimerini.com/news/1178487/four-more-ethnic-greeks-dead-in-ukraine/
Headline: Greek PM condemns the killing of ethnic Greeks in Ukraine

Description: In Berlin, Germany, Minister of Transport Volker Wissing announced that the government would ban Russian aircraft from German airspace and suspended air traffic to Russia for seven days, in line with the same approach adopted by other European countries in response to Russian invasion of Ukraine, Zeit reported. Source link : https://www.zeit.de/politik/ausland/2022-02/bundesverkehrsminister-bereitet-schliessung-des-deutschen-luftraums-vor
Headline: German Minister of Transport announces ban of Russian aircraft from German airspace

Description: In Amsterdam, Noord-Holland region, Netherlands, Prime Minister Mark Rutte rejected the proposals to ban the import of Russian oil gas to the European Union. Rutte stated that Europe should become less dependent on it but a ban would have enormous consequences. He also ruled out a no-fly zone over Ukraine and a military intervention by North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) in the Ukraine-Russia war, Dutch News reported. Source link : https://www.dutchnews.nl/news/2022/03/rutte-ban-on-russian-oil-would-have-enormous-consquences-for-europe/
Headline: Dutch PM rejects proposals to ban the import of Russian oil gas to the EU

Headline: Ukrainian President recalls the ambassador to Georgia

Description: In Kyiv, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine,  President Volodymyr Zelensky recalled the Ambassador to Kyrgyzstan over the alleged justification of Russian invasion of Ukraine by Kyrgyz officials, Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty reported. Source link : https://www.rferl.org/a/ukraine-kyrgyzstan-georgia-ambassadors/31730359.html
Headline: Ukrainian President recalls the Ambassador to Kyrgyzstan

Description:
In Moscow, Russia, Minister of Foreign Affairs Sergey Lavrov stated that it is important to stop Ukraine from turning into ‘Anti-Russia’. He added that is necessary to stop attempts by ‘placeholders’ in Kyiv to betray the vital interests of Ukrainian civilians, Tass reported.
 Source link :
Headline: Russian government intends to stop Ukraine from turning into ‘Anti-Russia’

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, the Third Secretary of the Embassy of the Kyrgyz Republic in Ukraine Ermek Kanybekov reported they had evacuated approximately 200 citizens from the country since Russian invasion began on 24 February 2022. They had evacuated 112 citizens to Hungary, 42 to Moldova, 47 to Poland, and 8 to Romania, Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty reported. Source link : https://rus.azattyk.org/a/31733917.html
Headline: Kyrgyzstan evacuates 200 citizens from Ukraine

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, presidential advisor Mykhailo Podolyak announced that Russia and Ukraine reached an agreement to set up humanitarian corridors in Ukraine for the evacuation of civilians and for supplying food and medical goods to the most affected areas, Kyiv Independent reported. Source link : https://kyivindependent.com/uncategorized/ukraine-russia-agree-to-set-up-humanitarian-corridors/
Headline: Russia and Ukraine agree to set up humanitarian corridors in Ukraine

Description:
In Chisinau, Moldova, Prime Minister Natalia Gavrilita stated that over 120,000 Ukrainian refugees had entered the country. She also urged other European Union (EU) members to support the refugees, Al Jazeera reported.
 Source link : https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/3/6/moldova-seeks-us-support-over-ukraine-war-refugees
Headline: Moldovan government briefs on Ukrainian refugees in the country

Description: In Minsk, Belarus, Border Guard Service Chief Yegor Zhigalo announced that Ukrainian citizens have entered the country. Around 140 Ukrainians crossed border and entered the country after the Russian invasion of Ukraine from 24 February 2022, Interfax reported. Source link :
Headline: Belarusian Border Guard Service Chief reports Ukrainians crossing border

Description:
In Moscow, Russia, Presidential Spokesperson Dmitry Peskov stated that President Vladimir Putin was not willing to hold direct talks with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. He stated that the ongoing negotiations between the countries’ representatives were sufficient, Korrespondent reported.
 Source link : https://korrespondent.net/ukraine/politics/4454140-v-kremle-ne-khotiat-perehovorov-putyna-s-zelenskym
Headline: Russian spokesperson briefs that the president refuses to hold direct talks with Ukrainian president

Description:
In Chisinau, Moldova, Prime Minister Natalia Gavrilita stated that there were no signs that the Russian invasion of Ukraine would expand to Moldova, despite the existence of the Russian Separatist region of Transnistria on the Moldova-Ukraine border, IPN reported.
 Source link : https://www.ipn.md/en/pm-gavrilita-no-signs-ukraine-war-could-spill-over-to-7965_1088329.html
Headline: Moldavan PM states there no signs that Russian invasion would expand to Moldova

Description:
In Minsk, Belarus, President Alexander Lukashenko, in a telephonic conversation, discussed economic cooperation with the Crown Prince of the United Arab Emirates (UAE) Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan. The leaders also discussed plans to boost energy security cooperation and the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Reform reported.
 Source link : https://reform.by/302006-lukashenko-pogovoril-s-naslednym-princem-abu-dabi
Headline: Belarusian President discusses economic cooperation with Crown Prince of UAE

Description:
In Kyiv, Ukraine, the government confirmed the successful evacuation of 48,000 civilians from Kyiv, Sumy and Enerhodar in Donetsk via a Russia-backed humanitarian corridor, Kyiv Independent reported
 Source link : https://kyivindependent.com/national/ukraine-evacuates-48000-people-many-still-in-dire-conditions-amid-russian-attacks/
Headline: Ukraine confirms evacuation of civilians from several towns in the country

Description:
In Kyiv, Ukraine, the government confirmed the successful evacuation of 5,000 civilians from Sumy and Poltava via a Russia-backed humanitarian corridor, Euro News reported.
 Source link : https://ru.euronews.com/2022/03/09/ukraine-attempted-evacuation
Headline: Ukraine confirms evacuation of civilians from Sumy

Description:
In Moscow, Russia, the government announced that deals with countries who have imposed sanctions on Russia will require special permission, Reuters reported. Countries such as United States, United Kingdom, Japan, European Union countries, among others imposed several sanctions against Russia after it invaded Ukraine.
 Source link :
Headline: Russian government mandates special permission for deals with countries that have imposed sanctions

Description:
In Kyiv, Ukraine, Chief of Presidential Staff Andriy Yermak said that Israel is a mediator in Russia-Ukraine peace talks, according to the Chief of Presidential Staff. He added that the Ukrainian government is constantly in touch with the Israeli head of the National Security council, Eyal Hulata.
 Source link :
Headline: Ukraine confirms Israel is mediating Russia-Ukraine peace talks

Description:
In Kyiv, Ukraine, Minister of Foreign Affairs Dmytro Kuleba and Croatian Minister of Foreign Affairs Gordan Grlic Radman discussed the sanctions on Russia and supported the idea of increasing the pressure on the country to end the invasion of Ukraine, according to the Minister of Foreign Affairs.
 Source link :
Headline: Ukrainian and Croatian Ministers of Foreign Affairs support increasing sanctions on Russia

Description:
In Kyiv, Ukraine, Head of the President’s office Kyrylo Tymoshenko said that around 20,000 civilians left the city in private cars using a humanitarian corridor. He added that around 570 cars have reached Zaporizhzhia Oblast, Interfax reported.
 Source link :
Headline: Thousands of civilians leave Mariupol via a humanitarian corridor

Description:
In Kyiv, Ukraine, the government agreed upon on nine humanitarian corridors to evacuate civilians from four Ukrainian regions. Residents of Donetsk, Kharkiv, Kyiv and Sumy will be evacuated via these corridors, Interfax reported.
 Source link :
Headline: Ukraine and Russia agree on nine humanitarian corridors to evacuate civilians

Description: In Sofia, Bulgaria, President Rumen Radev announced that Bulgaria was ready to take in refugees from Ukraine, adding that the Bulgarian government will transport refugees to Bulgaria in light of the deteriorating security situation in Ukraine, following the launch of a Russian military offensive against the country, Bulgarian National Radio reported. Source link : https://bnr.bg/en/post/101606057/president-rumen-radev-bulgaria-is-open-to-all-refugees-from-ukraine
Headline: Bulgarian President announces readiness to accept Ukrainian refugees

Description: In Brussels, Belgium, European Union (EU) agreed to a list of sanctions against Russia over its recognition of the independence of Ukraine’s separatist Donetsk People's Republic (DPR) and Luhansk People's Republic (LPR) territories. The measures would target Russia’s access to capital and financial markets and services, banks, public officials and trade with Donetsk People's Republic (DPR)/Luhansk People's Republic (LPR), DW reported. Source link : https://www.dw.com/en/ukraine-crisis-eu-agrees-to-russia-sanctions-package/a-60870787
Headline: European Union agrees to Russia's sanctions

Description: In Brussels, Belgium, Georgian President Salome Zurabishvili met with European Union (EU) leaders Charles Michel and Ursula von der Leyen. They discussed the war in Ukraine and also about situation in Georgia. They also discussed about Ukrainian and Moldovan European integration, and cyber and hybrid warfare cooperation, Civil reported. Source link : https://civil.ge/archives/476532
Headline: Georgian President meets EU leaders to discuss about war in Ukraine

Description: In Brussels, Belgium, European Union (EU) imposed sanctions in response to Russia's invasion of Ukraine. It suspended the broadcasting of Russia's state-owned media outlets Russia Today (RT) and Sputnik in the EU member states, Council of the European Union reported. Source link : https://www.consilium.europa.eu/en/press/press-releases/2022/03/02/eu-imposes-sanctions-on-state-owned-outlets-rt-russia-today-and-sputnik-s-broadcasting-in-the-eu/
Headline: European Union impose sanctions on Russian owned media outlets

Description: In Vienna, Austria, 26 countries voted in support of the resolution of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) on safety, security and safeguards implications of Russia’s military invasion in Ukraine. China and Russia voted against the resolution. India, Pakistan, Senegal, Vietnam and South Africa abstained from voting, Republic World reported. Source link : https://www.republicworld.com/world-news/russia-ukraine-crisis/iaea-passes-resolution-deploring-russias-attack-on-ukraine-india-abstains-from-voting-articleshow.html
Headline: Countries support IAEA resolution criticising Russia’s military invasion

Description: In London, England province, United Kingdom, the government imposed sanctions on Russian oligarchs Alisher Usmanov and Igor Shuvalov, after Russia's invaded Ukraine. The sanctioned entities were subject to an assets freeze and travel ban, Independent reported. Source link : https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/politics/russia-ukraine-usmanov-shuvalov-sanctions-b2028076.html
Headline: United Kingdom government imposes sanctions on Russian oligarchs

Description:
In London, England, United Kingdom (UK), independent Opposition Leader Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya met United Kingdom (UK) Member of Parliament (MP) James Cleverly to discuss the Russian invasion of Ukraine. The leaders also discussed humanitarian responses to Ukraine, according to Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya’s official Twitter account.
 Source link : https://twitter.com/Tsihanouskaya/status/1501606843122081797
Headline: Belarusian opposition leader meets UK MP to discuss the Russian invasion of Ukraine

Description: In Tbilisi, Georgia, Prime Minister Irakli Garibashvili discussed the war in Ukraine and the international peace efforts with Belgian Prime Minister Alexander De Croo  over a phone call. Garibashvili also spoke about its European Union accession application, Agenda reported. Source link : https://agenda.ge/en/news/2022/590
Headline: Georgian PM and Belgian counterpart discuss over international peace efforts and EU accession application

Description: In Kyiv city, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, the State Border Service introduced a number of additional restrictions in border areas with Belarus including bans on remaining in the area at night, the presence of foreigners, photography and transportation of border guards, Reform reported. Source link : https://reform.by/298550-ukraina-vvodit-ogranichenija-na-prigranichnyh-territorijah-s-belarusju
Headline: Ukraine's border agency introduces new restrictions in the border areas

Description: In Kyiv city, Kyiv, Ukraine, the government issued a NOTAM (Notice to Airmen), restricting all civilian flights in the airspace of Kyiv, Dnipro, Lviv, Odesa, Simferopol and Kyiv region, Pravda reported. Source link : https://www.pravda.com.ua/rus/news/2022/02/24/7325249/
Headline: Ukrainian government issues a NOTAM over its airspace

Description: In Prague, Hlavní město Praha region, Czech Republic, Prime Minister Petr Fiala stated that Russian Armed Forces could attack Ukraine 'at any moment'. He added that a peaceful resolution of tensions over the Russian military build-up on the Ukraine-Russia border was diminishing, Radio Prague International reported. Source link : https://english.radio.cz/czech-pm-russia-may-attack-ukraine-any-moment-8742534
Headline: Czech Prime Minister announces Russia can attack Ukraine at any moment

Description: In Warsaw, Mazowieckie region, Poland, Prime Minister’s Office reported queues of several kilometres with tens of thousands of Ukrainians on the border trying to enter Poland, the First News reported. Source link : https://www.thefirstnews.com/article/long-queues-at-polish-border-as-thousands-flee-ukraine-28243
Headline: Polish Prime Minister reports on Ukrainians trying to enter Poland

Description: In Bratislava, Slovakia, the Parliament passed an amendment to the Slovak Cyber Security Act. The act was aimed at allowing the National Security Authority (NBÚ) to shut down websites with malicious content. Potentially leading to cyber security incidents in light of Russian invasion of Ukraine, Spectator reported. Source link : https://spectator.sme.sk/c/22852644/news-digest-ec-president-von-der-leyen-will-arrive-to-slovakia-to-discuss-the-situation-in-ukraine.html
Headline: Slovakian Parliament amends Cyber Security Act

Description: In Warsaw, Mazowieckie region, Poland, President Andrzej Sebastian Duda met with Canadian Prime Minister Justin Pierre James Trudeau to discuss the war in Ukraine and humanitarian response. Duda called on Canada and the United States to contribute to post-war recovery funds, the First News reported. Source link : https://www.thefirstnews.com/article/duda-says-he-hopes-for-eu-us-and-canadian-recovery-funds-for-ukraine-28641
Headline: Polish President meets Canadian PM to discuss the war in Ukraine

Description: In Warsaw, Mazowieckie region, Poland, the head of Polish Oil Mining and Gas Extraction (Polskie Górnictwo Naftowe i Gazownictwo: PGNiG) refused to pay for Russian Gas in rubles. Russia demanded the payments in rubles after facing unprecedented sanctions on Moscow over its war in Ukraine, the Moscow Times reported. Source link : https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2022/03/24/poland-refuses-to-pay-for-russian-gas-in-rubles-a77073
Headline: Poland refuses to pay for Russian gas in rubles

Description: In Warsaw, Mazowieckie, Poland, United States President Joe Biden met Ukraine's Minister of Foreign Affairs Dmytro Kuleba and Minister of Defence Oleksii Reznikov. The ministers made a trip out of Ukraine as a possible sign of growing confidence in the fightback against Russian forces, Eye Witness News reported. Source link : https://ewn.co.za/2022/03/26/biden-meets-top-ukrainian-ministers-in-warsaw
Headline: US President meets Ukrainian ministers in Poland's Warsaw

Description: In Nicosia, Cyprus, Minister of Finance Constantinos Petrides stated that the government supported all European Union proposals for sanctions against Russia. The statement came after Russia's invasion of Ukraine, some of the sanctions included cutting off Russia from the Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication (SWIFT) banking system, refuting some domestic and international media reports, Cyprus Mail reported. Source link : https://cyprus-mail.com/2022/02/26/cyprus-did-not-oppose-any-sanctions-against-russia-says-finance-minister/
Headline: Cypriot Minister of Finance support EU proposals for sanctions against Russia

Description: In Nicosia, Cyprus, Commissioner for Citizens' Panayiotis Sentonas reported sending of the first convoy of humanitarian aid from Cyprus to Ukraine. The announced aid was under the consultation with the European Union civil protection mechanism that would set off on 8 March 2022. The announced aid consisted of pharmaceuticals worth EUR700,000, food donations, and civil defence equipment including tents, Cyprus Mail reported. Source link : https://cyprus-mail.com/2022/03/02/aid-from-cyprus-to-ukraine-to-leave-on-tuesday/
Headline: Cypriot Commissioner for Citizens' announces humanitarian aid for Ukraine

Description: In Nicosia, Cyprus, Ministry of Foreign Affairs spokesperson Demetris Demetriou stated that the government is still trying to evacuate its civilians. He reported around 50 Cypriot citizens in Ukraine and 10 Ukrainians with permanent residence in Cyprus would be evacuated. He further said that the evacuation of individual citizens was difficult due to the security situation, Cyprus Mail reported. Source link : https://cyprus-mail.com/2022/03/03/cyprus-still-working-to-evacuate-all-of-its-citizens-from-ukraine/
Headline: Cypriot Ministry of Foreign Affairs spokesperson reports on evacuating civilians from Ukraine

Description: In Ankara, Turkey, President Andrzej Sebastian Duda met with Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan to discuss the war in Ukraine. The meeting was to discuss assistance from the international community to accommodate the large flows of Ukrainian refugees that entered Poland, the First News reported. Source link : https://www.thefirstnews.com/article/duda-calls-for-international-help-in-ukrainian-refugee-crisis-28830
Headline: Polish President meets Turkish President to discuss the war in Ukraine

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, the Minister of Foreign Affairs of Poland Zbigniew Rau, Minister of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine Dmytro Kuleba, and the United Kingdom's Secretary of State Liz Truss announced they will sign the previously announced trilateral memorandum of cooperation, aimed at ensuring stability in Ukraine amid Russian military aggression, the First News reported. Source link : https://www.thefirstnews.com/article/polish-ukrainian-british-fms-announce-cooperation-accord-27956
Headline: Poland, Ukraine and UK sign trilateral memorandum of cooperation

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy stated Ukraine’s readiness to hold any format of talks with Russian government officials to reduce ongoing military tensions, but criticised Russia’s perceived unwillingness to enter into a real conversation, President Government reported. Source link : https://www.president.gov.ua/news/ukrayina-gotova-do-bud-yakogo-formatu-peremovin-iz-rosiyeyu-72953?fbclid=IwAR2bFv3sCLAewhHUvlfhSBgpqHwztDxVIviqrlnJ74Dzn-G4JIBEd8CPOGE
Headline: Ukraine’s President plans to meet Russian government officials

Description: In Tbilisi, Georgia, Minister of Foreign Affairs David Zalkalian met with Ukrainian counterpart Dmytro Kuleba to discuss Russia's military aggression towards Ukraine, agreeing on the need to intensify diplomacy through bilateral and multiracial formats ​to de-escalate the situation, Agenda reported. Source link : https://agenda.ge/en/news/2022/378
Headline: Georgian Minister of Foreign Affairs meets Ukrainian counterpart to discuss Russia-Ukraine aggression

Description: In Moscow, the Russian Federation, Russian Ministry of Defence confirmed the successful evacuation of over 720 civilians from the war. Civilians were evacuated from the Ukrainian City of Sumy to the settlement of Poltava, located at the Russia-Ukraine state border. A Russian-backed humanitarian corridor evacuated Indian, Chinese, Jordanian, and Tunisian citizens, CNN reported. Source link : https://edition.cnn.com/europe/live-news/ukraine-russia-putin-news-03-08-22/h_4e74e92c810d9f863df0789637e05001
Headline: Russian Ministry of Defence evacuate of civilians from Ukraine

Description: In Moscow, Russian Federation, Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak threatened to halt natural gas deliveries to Europe. The deliveries via the Gazprom-owned Nord Stream 1, in retaliation for international sanctions would be halted, CNN reported. Source link : https://edition.cnn.com/europe/live-news/ukraine-russia-putin-news-03-08-22/h_25b31b96441bb005da566b39321d44fe
Headline: Russian Deputy PM threatens to halt natural gas deliveries to Europe

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, President Volodymyr Oleksandrovych Zelenskyy dismissed Kirovohrad governor Maria Chorna and appointed Kropyvnytskyi mayor Andriy Raykovich to the post, Kypur reported. Source link : https://www.kypur.net/mariya-chorna-u-mene-vse-ok-ya-zalyshayusya-v-oblasti/
Headline: Ukraine's President appoints Kropyvnytskyi mayor as Kirovohrad governor

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, authorities banned wheat exports, in the context of the Russian invasion. Ukraine was a major wheat import source for Egypt, AP News reported. Source link : https://apnews.com/article/russia-ukraine-business-europe-global-trade-38159046f7744b8c660ee7582dd392bf
Headline: Ukrainian authorities bans exporting wheat products

Description: In Mariupol, Donetsk region, Ukraine, the Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE) began an evacuation operation with Greek officials that the was underway in Ukraine's Mariupol. The operation was to evacuate Greek Consul General Manolis Androulakis and local OSCE staff, Ekathimerini reported. Source link : https://www.ekathimerini.com/news/1179820/operation-to-evacuate-greek-consul-general-in-mariupol-underway/
Headline: OSCE begin an operation to evacuate Greek Consul General from Ukraine in Ukraine's Mariupol

Description: In Kyiv, Ukraine, North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) Secretary General Jens Stoltenberg stated that the NATO allies are stepping up their support for Ukraine. NATO plans to provide arms supplies and humanitarian aid, as well as through billions of dollars in aid to its economy, Libya Akhbar reported. Source link : https://www.libyaakhbar.com/news/1812387.html
Headline: NATO allies support Ukraine through humanitarian aid

Description: At the Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine, Kyiv, Ukraine, the Ukrainian parliament appointed Mykola Solskyy as the new Minister of Agriculture. The appointment was after Roman Leshchenko stepped down from the post, BBC Monitoring reported. Source link : https://monitoring.bbc.co.uk/product/c203c999
Headline: Ukrainian Parliament appoints new Minister of Agriculture

Description: In Moscow, Russian Federation, President Vladimir Putin signed a law against citizens found guilty of spreading fake news about the work of officials abroad. The civilians will be sentenced to up to 15 years in jail, Interfax news agency reported. Source link : https://www.voanews.com/a/latest-developments-in-ukraine-march-25/6501043.html
Headline: Russian President signs a against civilians spreading fake news

Description: In Moscow, Russian Federation, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani met with Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Russian Federation Sergey Lavrov, where the talks dealt with the situation in Ukraine. There were other unspecified diplomatic talks during the meet, Al Watan Qatar reported. Source link : https://www.al-watan.com/opinion-news-all
Headline: Russian Minister of Foreign Affairs meets with Qatar's Minister of Foreign Affairs

Description: In Athens, Attica region, Greece, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs stated that the Greek Embassy in Kyiv was being evacuated and that the ambassador requested and received approval to leave Ukraine. Consulates in Mariupol and Odesa would, for the time being, remain in operation, Keep Talking Greece reported. Source link : https://www.keeptalkinggreece.com/2022/02/25/greece-evacuates-embassy-in-kiev-consulates-in-mariupol-odessa-still-in-operation/
Headline: Greek Ministry of Foreign Affairs evacuates ambassador from Ukraine

Description: In Athens, Attica region, Greece, the government delivered 14 tons of humanitarian aid to Ukraine. Ukrainian Commander of the Joint Forces of the Armed Forces Lieutenant-General Serhiy Nayev stated that the cargo consisted of medicines, food, and clothing, Greek Reporter reported. Source link : https://greekreporter.com/2022/03/21/greece-delivers-humanitarian-aid-ukraine/
Headline: Greece delivers humanitarian aid to Ukraine

Description: In Sofia, Bulgaria, Ministry of Foreign Affairs announced the expulsion of 10 Russian diplomats. They were considered for 'activities inconsistent with the Vienna Convention on Diplomatic Relations'. The decision was made against Russian President Vladimir Putin to stop the war against Ukraine, Al Wasat reported. Source link : http://alwasat.ly/news/international/352905
Headline: Bulgaria's Ministry of Foreign Affairs expel Russian diplomats

Description: In Munich, Bayern region, Germany, during the Munich Security Conference Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyi called for a summit of the signatories of the Budapest Memorandum on Security Assurances, in an attempt to de-escalate the conflict in eastern Ukraine. Zelenskyi threatened to withdraw from the memorandum otherwise, RBC reported. Source link : https://www.rbc.ru/politics/19/02/2022/621108ac9a7947f316c3b93e?utm_source=facebook&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=preview&fbclid=IwAR0yErfIwpDzJoY57LO3QLFP4BFVvMBaMBdDJoqMZisdIVZTWrqzWGZMiqU
Headline: Ukraine’s President call for a summit of the Budapest Memorandum on Security Assurances in Germany's Munich

Description: In London, England province, United Kingdom, Prime Minister Alexander Boris de Pfeffel Johnson stated that the United States and United Kingdom could cut off Russian companies’ access to trading in USD and GBP in the event of an invasion of Ukraine, Reuters reported. Source link : https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/west-would-cut-russian-companies-access-dollars-if-ukraine-is-invaded-uks-2022-02-20/
Headline: United Kingdom's PM states US and UK can cut Russian companies’ access to trading

Description: In Amsterdam, Noord-Holland region, Netherlands, Ministry of Foreign Affairs stated that the Dutch embassy in Kyiv (Ukraine) would temporarily relocate to the city of Lviv (Ukraine) for security reasons, amid rising concerns of Russian military invasion, NL Times reported. Source link : https://nltimes.nl/2022/02/20/netherlands-moves-ukraine-embassy-lviv-security-concerns-kyiv
Headline: Dutch Ministry of Foreign Affairs states to relocate its embassy in Ukraine to the city of Lviv for security reasons

Description: In Brussels, Belgium, European Union officials stated that the bloc would consider new restrictive measures to close loopholes on existing sanctions against Belarus. And in the event of Belarus participating in Russia's potential military incursions into Ukraine, Reuters reported. Source link : https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/eu-mulls-new-measures-against-belarus-withdrew-some-staff-kyiv-sources-2022-02-18/
Headline: EU considers new measures to close loopholes on Belarus

Description: In Brussels, Belgium, European Commission's President Ursula Gertrud von der Leyen and the European Council's President Charles Michel issued a statement condemning Russia's recognition of Ukraine's Luhansk and Donetsk oblasts as independent entities and sending troops to these areas a violation of Ukraine's sovereignty and international law, Europa reported. Source link : https://ec.europa.eu/commission/presscorner/detail/en/statement_22_1281
Headline: European Commission condemns Russia's recognition of independence of Ukraine's LPR and DPR regions.

Description: In Düsseldorf, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germany, the Green party (Grüne) urged the federal government and the federal state North Rhine-Westphalia to help Ukrainian refugees, after Russian troops invaded Ukraine, urging municipalities to prepare respective protection and security facilities, Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung reported. Source link : https://www.faz.net/agenturmeldungen/dpa/gruene-nrw-und-bund-muessen-gefluechteten-aus-ukraine-helfen-17829815.html
Headline: Green party urges government to help Ukrainian refugees in Germany's Düsseldorf

Description: In Berlin, Germany, the Federal Foreign Office issued a travel warning that restricted civilians to travel to Russia and Ukrainian border areas. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs announced limited use of non-Russian credit cards in Russia and restrictions on air travel between Russia and most European countries, Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung reported. Source link : https://www.faz.net/aktuell/politik/ausland/ukraine-liveticker-ukrainer-sollen-russland-verlassen-17804564.html
Headline: German Federal Office restrict civilians travel to Russia and Ukrainian border areas

Description: In Madrid, Spain, the Minister of Foreign Affairs, José Manuel Albares Bueno reported the evacuation of Spanish ambassador and diplomatic staff from Ukraine. Along with them there were around 100 Spanish citizens during the Russian invasion of Ukraine who were evacuated as well, Infobae reported. Source link : https://www.infobae.com/america/agencias/2022/02/25/espana-evacua-de-ucrania-a-su-embajadora-y-a-un-centenar-de-ciudadanos/
Headline: Spanish government evacuates ambassador and diplomatic staff from Ukraine

Description: In Madrid, Spain, Ministry of Foreign Affairs reported that 150 citizens were evacuated from Ukraine, including the Spanish ambassador and the remaining diplomatic staff, after they arrived in Spain, El Diario reported. Source link : https://www.eldiario.es/canariasahora/sociedad/150-espanoles-evacuados-ucrania-llegaran-proxima-madrugada-espana_1_8787626.html
Headline: Spanish Ministry of Foreign Affairs report on evacuation of ambassador and civilians

Description: In Amsterdam, Noord-Holland region, Netherlands, Prime Minister Mark Rutte announced that the government would be generous in offering shelter to asylum seekers from Ukraine. Adding that first asylum seekers had already been sheltered in the Harskamp in Ede. The Netherlands did everything in its power to help Poland that was receiving most refugees, NL Times reported. Source link : https://nltimes.nl/2022/03/03/netherlands-scaling-plans-ukrainian-refugees-rutte-address-ukrainian-people
Headline: Dutch Prime Minister announces shelter to asylum seekers from Ukraine

Description: In Brussels, Belgium, the European Union presented its plan for the reduction of Europe’s dependency on Russian natural gas supply. Envisioning the decrease in Russian gas procurement by two-thirds in 2022 and its complete halt by 'well before 2030', CNN reported. Source link : https://edition.cnn.com/europe/live-news/ukraine-russia-putin-news-03-08-22/h_3b6d803c18e3257f0882496d0582c98d
Headline: EU presents the plan of Europe’s dependency on Russian natural gas supply

Description: In London, England province, United Kingdom, Prime Minister Alexander Boris de Pfeffel Johnson announced a programe to provide housing facilities to Ukraine refugees. Seized Russian oligarch homes in UK would be used as homes for the refugees, Roya News reported. Source link :
Headline: UK provide housing to Ukrainian refugees

Description: In Brussels, Belgium, European Union foreign affairs ministers are expected to discuss new sanctions on Russia. Poland and the Baltic states continued its restrictions as Russian Forces continued their invasion of Ukraine leaving a mounting civilian death toll, EU Observer reported. Source link : https://euobserver.com/world/154545
Headline: EU foreign affairs ministers are plan discussing new sanctions against Russia

Description: In Prague, Czech Republic, Minister of Interior Vít Rakušan stated that over 200,000 Ukrainian refugees had entered Czech Republic since the launch of Russian invasion of Ukraine. He added that the government had issued around 108,000 humanitarian visas to refugees till 10 March 2022, Czech Radio reported. Source link : https://english.radio.cz/number-ukranian-refugees-czechia-nearing-200000-8744387
Headline: Czech Minister of Interior briefs on Ukrainian refugees

Description: In Vilnius, Lithuania, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs confirmed that the government had expelled four diplomats of the Embassy of Russia after declaraing them 'undesriable persons'. The ministry stated that the decision was taken in solidarity with Ukraine, DELFI reported. Source link : https://www.delfi.lt/en/politics/lithuania-expels-four-russian-diplomats-in-solidarity-with-ukraine.d?id=89742105
Headline: Lithuania expels four Russian diplomats
